Airdev took a structured and methodical approach to the project, beginning with detailed scoping of each milestone to ensure clarity in requirements and alignment with business goals. The development process included building a functional MVP marketplace that allowed lawyers and companies to connect efficiently, while also laying the groundwork for future scalability. Airdev assigned a team of 6–10 professionals, including developers, designers, and project managers, to execute the project with precision. One of the key strengths of the engagement was their disciplined project management approach, which included weekly deliverables, clearly defined milestones, and dedicated time windows for review, approval, testing, and post-launch support. This structured workflow ensured that the client remained actively involved throughout the process and that feedback was incorporated effectively at every stage. As a result of the engagement, HeyCounsel successfully achieved over 400 signups within six months of launch while maintaining zero downtime, demonstrating both the stability and effectiveness of the platform. Airdev’s attention to detail and commitment to delivering a high-quality product were evident in the reliability and performance of the final solution. The client highlighted the team’s positive attitude, responsiveness, and technical capability as standout qualities, noting that working with them was a smooth and enjoyable experience. Additionally, Airdev’s ability to provide design and architectural guidance helped shape the product beyond just development, contributing to a more thoughtful and scalable platform. Review Text: Anthroware partnered with Specialty Sport Reps, an independent sales agency operating in the outdoor industry, to design and develop a website that would effectively represent the agency’s brand, improve visibility, and provide a centralized platform for partners and vendors to access key resources. The client’s primary objective was to create a professional digital presence that could showcase their capabilities and help potential vendors better understand their business, while also offering existing partners an efficient way to access important assets. To achieve this, Anthroware began the engagement with a series of discovery and strategy sessions focused on deeply understanding the agency’s identity, values, and long-term vision. This process went beyond basic design requirements and involved defining the company’s persona and refining its mission statement, ensuring that the final product would accurately reflect the brand’s positioning within the industry. The team conducted multiple creative sessions and design reviews, allowing for continuous alignment between the client’s vision and the evolving product. The project team consisted of key roles including the CEO, a creative director, and a UX designer, all of whom contributed to shaping a cohesive and polished final outcome. Throughout the engagement, Anthroware maintained strong communication practices through well-structured virtual meetings that were concise, organized, and easy to follow, making the process accessible even for a client without deep technical expertise. The final website significantly improved the agency’s brand awareness, enabling potential vendors to easily research the business and understand its value proposition. Additionally, the platform enhanced operational efficiency by providing clients with streamlined access to important resources and links, ultimately improving productivity. One of the standout aspects of the collaboration was Anthroware’s ability to communicate complex design concepts in a simple and understandable way, empowering the client to confidently manage and evolve the platform after delivery. While the client noted that timelines could always feel long when aiming for a high-quality product, the end result justified the investment of time and effort. Overall, Anthroware delivered a highly effective, user-friendly, and visually aligned website that positioned Specialty Sport Reps strongly within its industry and supported its continued growth.

Review Text: Fusion Tech partnered with Spillt, a social app company, to develop and continuously enhance its mobile application across iOS and Android platforms. The primary goal of the engagement was to accelerate product development while ensuring that the application remained scalable, reliable, and user-friendly as it evolved. The development team worked using React Native for cross-platform functionality and leveraged PostgreSQL via Supabase for backend data management. Starting with core features such as login flows, a news feed, and push notifications, the team progressively added more advanced functionalities over time, aligning with the product’s growth strategy. Fusion Tech assigned a focused team consisting of an engineer, a project manager, and QA specialists, ensuring both technical execution and quality assurance were consistently maintained. One of the key strengths of the collaboration was the structured project management approach, which included weekly meetings to review progress, clarify upcoming tasks, and address any issues. Real-time communication was handled through Slack, while ClickUp was used to track tasks and maintain visibility across development cycles. This disciplined workflow enabled the team to deliver features promptly and respond quickly to changing requirements. The application successfully launched and received strong positive feedback from users, particularly regarding its intuitive design and ease of use. The client highlighted that the code quality was consistently high, with rapid delivery cycles that supported continuous improvement of the product. Additionally, the project manager contributed significant value by proactively identifying usability challenges and suggesting improvements that enhanced the overall user experience. Fusion Tech exceeded expectations not only through technical capability but also through their thoughtful engagement with the product, ensuring that design and functionality aligned with user needs. The collaboration has remained ongoing, with the team continuing to add new features and support the app’s growth. Overall, Fusion Tech delivered a high-quality, scalable mobile application while maintaining excellent communication, responsiveness, and a strong focus on both performance and user experience.

Review Text: Fusion Tech partnered with CloudPano, a 360-degree photography and virtual touring platform, initially to improve the company’s user authentication system and later became a full-service development partner across the product. The first project focused on expanding login options beyond Facebook and Google authentication, allowing users to sign in through more flexible methods such as email. After successfully completing that initial work, Fusion Tech took on broader development responsibilities for the CloudPano application, supporting the team through an agile development model that included both large feature “epics” and smaller QA tasks. A major area of work was CloudPano Live, which integrated Twilio’s Video API to allow users to hold live video conferencing sessions inside virtual tours. The team also helped design and implement the UX/UI for this feature, making it a significant value-add for the platform. Beyond video calling, Fusion Tech built a coordinate broadcasting system that lets users stream their position within a 360-degree space during calls, improving interactivity and collaboration. They also developed enterprise functionality that supports property marketing to broker houses and real estate customers, along with a Google Street View integration that allows users to publish tours directly from CloudPano to Google Street View. Their work substantially increased development velocity, moving the client from roughly 7–10 complexity points per week to around 30, effectively tripling product progress. Fusion Tech’s team included developers, a project manager, a QA specialist, and a designer, all of whom contributed to a transparent and collaborative workflow. Communication through Slack, Google Meet, and project management tools kept progress visible and organized. The client valued the team’s strong technical competency, noting that they could be trusted to handle complex tasks or find effective solutions independently. Overall, Fusion Tech delivered high-quality development support, accelerated product execution, improved feature planning, and helped CloudPano build revenue-driving capabilities with strong expected ROI.

linkedin company about:
Diode Ventures is an infrastructure developer for the commercial, industrial and technology sectors, specializing in clean energy and data centers assets. Diode Ventures is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Black & Veatch with a global presence in over 100 countries. Visit www.diodeventures.com for more information.

About us
We combine planning, business strategy and technology into a unified discipline powerful enough to propel companies forward.

Projects

About us
Slide 2 of 4.
Diode Ventures® is a team of diverse and experienced professionals, with the ability to take a project from a conceptual need to a constructed reality. Diode is backed by over a century of experience as a Black & Veatch company that was founded by Black & Veatch professionals.

Although we are not exclusive with Black & Veatch as our EPC partner, our relationship with them grants us access to their in-house expertise, global network, and superior knowledge gained from decades of experience in Alternative & Renewable Energy, Distributed Infrastructure, Telecommunications and Data Centers.

Our goal is to be a partner to our clients. We use a client-centric development model to identify the asset type, region, and size of projects, then Diode either develops the project from scratch or identifies projects in progress to meet their needs.

Our mission and vision
Our Mission is to streamline the asset development process by providing client-centric, turnkey infrastructure solutions.
‍
Our Vision is to become trusted development partners who preserve and connect the world with innovative solutions in sustainable and critical infrastructure fields. ​

We want to reinvent the model of a typical developer by making it easier for companies to deliver new infrastructure assets that will grow their business faster. We work to help clients meet their project requirements so that they can focus on their core business.

Our contributions
Million tree challenge reforestation partner logo
Diode is committed to giving back to the communities we live and work in, see more about our work in 2025 here. We encourage our team to get involved in the organizations they are passionate about, which is why we have Volunteer Paid Time Off (VPTO) for our professionals to choose how they spend their time.

In addition to giving our time, we also contribute to organizations that align with our values, including sustainability. In 2022, Diode planted 10,000 trees in partnership with One Tree Planted in their Million Tree Challenge.

Our people
Diode Ventures started with a conversation.


Diode was founded based on the premise of a theme amongst leaders in the commercial, industrial (C&I), and technology space: When it came to developing and managing assets—whether to improve operational costs or meet demand—there was a desire for these companies to have a single entity to drive the process of development of the assets.
 
After a series of conversations with corporate customers in the technology, commercial and industrial space, Diode Ventures was created to provide clients with a full-service solution to develop and manage their infrastructure assets.

We are not a construction company. We are not an engineering firm. We are conductors that connect all of the dots on infrastructure projects.
‍
Our team is comprised of a mix of former Black & Veatch consultants, engineers, financiers, as well as industry originators and developers. We are all thinkers, doers and — most importantly — adapters. We are empowered with the information and tools we need to make a world-wide impact.

Mentor Mentee is a Tech Proficiency Platform built for service teams in automotive and transportation. 

We help organizations improve productivity and unlock capacity by giving leaders visibility into technician capability and progress. The result is faster time-to-productivity, more consistent output, and stronger profitability across the operation.

Train Faster • Retain Longer • Build ProfitAbout Us
Most service centers are capacity-constrained not by bays or equipment, but by which techs can do which work. We exist to solve that. Mentor Mentee provides a platform that accelerates technician proficiency that helps service operations unlock hidden capacity and improve labor efficiency.

Read How We Do It
Mentor Mentee App Used By Mentee in Auto Shop
Proud to Serve and Support
Our Mission: Empower People For Peak Success
Our Founding Story
Mentor Mentee began with lessons our founder, Lirel Holt, learned as a young technician working his way through college. The mentors who invested in him shaped his career and inspired decades of work supporting others in the automotive and collision industries.

Lirel went on to found CARSTAR and later co-found S/P2. Across those experiences, one challenge remained consistent: technicians needed structured career progression, and service leaders needed a practical way to track skill development. Mentor Mentee was created to track proficiency based on actual jobs completed, making technician growth visible and easier for teams to use effectively.

Who We Are Today
Today, Mentor Mentee is led by CEO Ron Helmer and a team focused on helping service teams operate more effectively. Shaped by Ron's background as a U.S. Navy veteran, his leadership emphasizes accountability, clarity, and putting people in the right position to succeed.

With a shared focus on operational excellence, Mentor Mentee continues to evolve alongside service teams. As a company, we're committed to building proficiency tracking that supports technician growth while improving how work gets done day to day.

Review Text: Crema partnered with Bloomscape, an online plant retailer, to deliver mobile app development and transition support for a customer care application across iOS and Android platforms. The engagement centered on acquiring and integrating Crema’s existing app (Vera) into Bloomscape’s ecosystem to enhance post-purchase customer support. Crema played a critical role in transferring ownership while ensuring continuity for an already established user base. The team included engineers, a product manager, and a designer who worked closely with Bloomscape stakeholders to refine features and align the app with business goals. Their collaborative approach enabled seamless communication through tools like Slack and Jira, allowing both teams to manage new feature development while addressing existing user needs. Despite aggressive timelines, Crema delivered the project on schedule and maintained a high standard of quality. The transition required managing a complex backlog of updates due to the app’s prior global footprint, but Crema’s deep familiarity with the product helped navigate these challenges effectively. The result was a smooth rollout of a customer care app that received positive user feedback and strengthened Bloomscape’s ability to support customers post-purchase. Crema’s responsiveness, ownership mindset, and strong collaboration made them feel like an extension of the internal team, ultimately contributing to a successful product integration and improved customer experience.

Review Text: Baytech Consulting partnered with SellYourBoards.com, an e-scrap refining company that buys circuit boards and other electronic waste, to replace its static website with a dynamic, data-driven web application. The client wanted to improve customer experience, reduce manual order-handling time, minimize operational bottlenecks, and implement only the highest-value features to control cost. Baytech developed an interactive platform connected to USPS’s API, allowing customers to create their own shipping labels and send scrap circuit boards directly to the company’s facility. In addition, Baytech created an employee portal that allowed internal staff to receive orders, mark them as sorted or complete, add invoice line items, input item weights, and calculate total customer payouts directly inside the system. Another major deliverable was a custom CMS that allowed administrators to manage the public price list, including adding or deleting items and categories, uploading item photos, and updating prices. The final platform improved customer experience, automated shipping label generation, reduced processing delays, enabled better order tracking, and decreased manual work for the team. Baytech delivered all items on time and responded quickly to bugs, often fixing issues within 30 minutes. The client especially appreciated the team’s communication, preparedness, and willingness to prototype before the first call, which showed a strong commitment to understanding the project. Overall, Baytech delivered a high-quality, operationally valuable platform that helped SellYourBoards.com modernize its workflow and scale its e-scrap buying process more efficiently.

Missions
Bank Holding Companies
BHCs offer benefits through diversification and economies of scale. They can access new markets while maintaining regulatory advantages. BHCs have greater flexibility to innovate, create synergies, enhance brand recognition, and optimize capital and liquidity.

Bank Acquisitions
Bank acquisitions  are a strategic avenue for financial institutions to rapidly enhance shareholder value by expanding their footprint, acquiring valuable talent and technology, and diversifying their customer base. These transactions offer the potential for significant revenue growth through cross-selling opportunities and the realization of operational efficiencies, ultimately strengthening the acquiring institution's competitive position and long-term profitability.

Credit Union Vehicles 
Credit unions offer a unique member-owned financial structure .Credit Union Service Organizations (CUSOs) can provide businesses with many of the immediate financial services they require, often with a tailored approach. Furthermore, CUSOs can represent a strategic pathway for a business to eventually transition into owning and operating their own chartered credit union, providing greater control over their banking relationships and fostering a financial ecosystem aligned with their specific goals and the needs of their stakeholders.   

Recapitalizations
Recapitalization is a powerful strategic tool for Financial Institutions Groups (FIG) seeking to optimize their balance sheets for regulatory compliance and enhanced investor confidence. Traditional public and private businesses can also leverage recapitalization to restructure debt, unlock growth capital, or defend against unwanted takeovers, ultimately aiming to improve financial flexibility and long-term sustainability in dynamic market conditions.

Liquidity and Equity
We strategically deploy our capital to support the growth and expansion of Financial Institutions Groups, as well as promising public and private enterprises. Our tailored liquidity solutions ensure you have the financial agility to seize opportunities, while our equity investments provide the resources necessary to achieve your long-term vision.

Acquisitions and Dispositions
Strategic planning for Acquisitions and Dispositions (M&A) is essential for FIG institutions and other companies considering an exit strategy or looking to expand through acquisitions. This involves a comprehensive evaluation of market conditions, potential synergies, valuation, and regulatory implications to ensure a successful transaction, whether it's divesting assets or acquiring new firms to achieve strategic growth objectives.

Home

Our Story
Our Story
Built in Houston.
Built From Operations.
TriArc didn't start with a fund. It started with operators.

Our Story
Most Firms Raise Capital First.
We Built Operations First.
TriArc is a vertically integrated multifamily investment firm built from the ground up by operators. The sequence matters — and it shapes every underwriting decision, every acquisition, and every asset we take on.

Joseph Bramante acquired his first apartment complex while still working as an engineer at ExxonMobil. When he went full-time into real estate, what followed was an expensive education: high vacancy, fraudulent insurance, asbestos, and the hard lessons that come from not yet knowing what you're doing. He learned this business from the inside out — not from a private equity desk.

The turning point came when he partnered with Carrie Breneman and Deborah Newsome. The two sisters had been running multifamily communities since the late 1980s — starting as leasing agents and working their way up through Lincoln Property Company, the Lynd Company, Sentinel Real Estate, and Gables Residential, eventually overseeing portfolios of 5,000 to 11,000 units. In 2012 they founded Kindred Residential, a third-party management firm built on the conviction that property management should be customized to the client's actual needs — not packaged and sold as a commodity.

Joseph hired Kindred to manage his first Houston property. What he found was a team that thought about assets the way an owner does — focused on what each property actually needed, honest about what the numbers showed, and aligned to the outcome rather than the fee. Four years and four properties later, in 2016, Kindred merged with Joseph's company and rebranded as TriArc Real Estate Partners — launching with 3,000 units already under management and a leadership team with four decades of combined operating experience.

In 2020, Robert Mitchell joined as a partner and TriArc Construction was formed — completing the platform. Bringing construction in-house wasn't about cost alone. It was about accountability, alignment, and real-time visibility into what renovations actually cost and which scopes actually produce returns. When the same team underwrites the deal, manages the renovation, and operates the asset, the incentives are fully aligned at every stage.

That origin is why "vertically integrated" isn't a marketing phrase for us. It's how we were built.

linkedin company about:
SynergyMed is a C-Corp medical device company with a proven technology.

The company is focused on the development of cancer treatment products for human, veterinary and research applications based on an innovative noninvasive ablation technology. Our technology is a platform technology that may be used as an alternative to surgery for the treatment of early-stage breast, head & neck, skin and lung cancers. It may also be used for pain relief in palliative care treatments.

The proven technology can locally ablate and destroy cancer tumors, precisely and effectively, without causing any damage to the surrounding healthy tissues. It is based on using specialized noninvasive electromagnetic applicators that are placed around the region of interest along with FDA/CE approved nanoparticles that are placed inside the tumor.  The device is controlled through the company’s own developed algorithms that optimize the ablation process while precisely tracking temperature and measuring the administered treatment dose.


The technology has been tested on mice with excellent results and is currently being tested on dogs for the veterinary market. The company is also planning its first-in-human studies. 

The company is currently seeking VC funding and strategic partnerships with large medical device corporations and hospital consortiums. The company is also accepting pre-orders of devices for R&D and veterinary applications.

Ferret Inspection Cameras for Gutter Cleaning, High Dusting & Hard-to-Reach Inspections

by Dallas Davis
on Mar 12 2026
When you're cleaning gutters or performing high dusting, visibility can be one of the biggest challenges. From the ground, it’s often difficult to know exactly where debris is hiding inside a gutter run or what dust buildup is sitting along rafters and beams. That’s where Ferret inspection cameras make a huge difference. These compact wireless cameras can be attached to extension poles, gutter cleaning poles, glow rods, or inspection rods, allowing you to see exactly what’s happening in hard-to-reach spaces while you work. By streaming live video directly to your smartphone, Ferret cameras let you inspect gutters, rafters, and ceiling structures in real time, helping you guide your tools more accurately and complete jobs faster. At Clean Direct, we carry two powerful options from Ferret Tools: Ferret Pro Inspection Camera Ferret Plus Inspection Camera Attach the Camera to Your Pole for Real-Time Cleaning Visibility One of the most useful features of Ferret cameras is their ability to be mounted to the poles you already use for cleaning or inspection. When attached to a pole, the camera can be raised into gutters, rooflines, rafters, or ceiling spaces so you can see exactly what you're working on while cleaning. This gives you the ability to: Locate packed debris inside gutters Find clogs in downspouts Inspect gutter guards for buildup Guide cleaning tools precisely Check roof edges and fascia areas Inspect rafters and beams during high dusting Instead of relying on guesswork, you can watch the cleaning process in real time on your phone and adjust your tools accordingly. Perfect for Gutter Cleaning Professionals For gutter cleaning companies, having the ability to see inside the gutter system while working can dramatically improve efficiency. A Ferret inspection camera lets you quickly identify where debris has accumulated and direct your cleaning tools to the exact location. Common gutter cleaning uses include: Inspecting gutters from the ground Locating hidden clogs Checking downspouts for blockages Viewing debris behind gutter guards Confirming gutters are fully cleared Capturing before-and-after visuals for customers When mounted to your cleaning pole, the camera acts like your eyes inside the gutter. Ideal for High Dusting and Overhead Cleaning High dusting often requires working around rafters, beams, pipes, ductwork, and elevated ledges where dust accumulates over time. In warehouses and commercial facilities, these areas can be difficult to inspect visually. With a Ferret camera attached to a high dusting pole, you can raise the camera into these spaces and inspect them before and after cleaning. This helps crews: Identify dust buildup on rafters Inspect structural beams and ledges View around ductwork and piping Guide cleaning tools accurately Verify cleanliness after the job is complete This level of visibility can make high dusting services far more precise and efficient. Active ETF Challenge
Protecting the Portfolio Manager’s Edge
Active ETFs are expanding rapidly, driven by investor demand for innovative strategies that capture alpha.

But the standard fully transparent ETF structure exposes every holding and trade daily — turning your IP into an open book. Copycat and predatory traders – now armed with AI – can quickly unravel a portfolio manager’s strategy.

Review Text: Black Airplane partnered with Arch-I-Tech Doors, Inc. to design and develop a highly complex, custom-built e-commerce platform tailored specifically for the commercial door and hardware distribution industry. The client required a robust solution beyond standard platforms like Shopify or WooCommerce due to the intricate nature of their product catalog, which includes components with up to 1,400 variations. The engagement began with a deep discovery phase, where Black Airplane invested significant time understanding the business model, product relationships, and customer behavior. This foundational work enabled them to architect a custom Laravel-based tech stack optimized for performance, scalability, and fast search functionality. One of the most critical deliverables was a custom data warehouse that structured complex product information in a way that allowed customers to easily navigate and retrieve relevant data. The platform also introduced advanced capabilities such as a drag-and-drop product assembly tool, enabling users to configure hardware components dynamically. Additionally, AI-driven features were integrated to assess customer knowledge levels during the purchasing process, allowing the system to provide contextual assistance and reduce the likelihood of incorrect purchases and returns. VR technology was also incorporated to enhance visualization and decision-making. Throughout the engagement, Black Airplane demonstrated strong project management practices, maintaining consistent communication through daily interactions, biweekly check-ins, and leadership accessibility. Their proactive approach, creativity, and ability to handle technical complexity resulted in highly positive feedback from beta users and internal stakeholders. Despite external challenges like the pandemic, the collaboration remained structured and transparent, with the platform progressing through multiple development phases toward launch.

Review Text: Something Borrowed Blooms, a fast-growing rent-and-return floral marketplace, engaged Small Planet to conduct a comprehensive audit of their technology infrastructure to address scalability concerns and future growth readiness. Following a sitewide outage during a product launch, the company recognized the need to evaluate its existing tech stack, which had been built on an open-source platform with custom logic to support its unique business model. The engagement involved a deep dive into the company’s systems, including backend architecture, workflows, and operational dependencies. Small Planet conducted structured kickoff sessions, daily alignment huddles, and extensive documentation using Confluence to map out current systems and identify bottlenecks. The team delivered a detailed report outlining both short-term fixes and long-term strategic recommendations aimed at enabling the company to scale efficiently and reliably. One of the most significant outcomes was providing the client with multiple viable technology pathways to support a 10x growth vision. The audit also gave internal stakeholders clarity on enterprise-level development practices, empowering better future decision-making. Small Planet’s ability to quickly understand and retain the complexities of the company’s highly customized model stood out, along with their strong communication, organization, and adaptability when collaborating with existing developers. Overall, the engagement equipped Something Borrowed Blooms with a clear roadmap to strengthen its infrastructure and support continued expansion. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0} :contentReference[oaicite:1]{index=1}

Reach-out Message: Hi Tim, Building a tiered portal to manage 600+ entities and external collaborators is a complex systems challenge. How did you structure data + workflows to maintain clarity while scaling decision-making across Bee Partners’ portfolio? Looking forward to connecting. Parag Google | Founder SoftwareBrio.com
Review Text: For Bee Partners, a pre-seed venture capital firm managing over 40 portfolio companies and hundreds of external collaborators, Crowdbotics developed a highly sophisticated tiered authenticated portal to streamline operations and decision-making. The core challenge was enabling a small internal team to efficiently manage complex relationships with over 600 entities, including founders, investors, service providers, and partners. Crowdbotics designed and built a scalable internal platform that evolved into a multi-tiered system allowing authenticated third parties to securely access the portal and contribute to various operational workflows. The platform leveraged technologies such as Django, PostgreSQL, React Native, and Heroku, and was developed using agile methodologies with 1–2 week sprint cycles. The system automated manual portfolio operations, enhanced visibility across companies, and introduced structured workflows that allowed external collaborators to self-select and assist with requests. The portal became mission-critical to Bee Partners’ daily operations, significantly improving decision-making efficiency and enabling scalable collaboration. Crowdbotics maintained strong project management through transparent communication, rapid feedback loops, and consistent delivery against defined objectives. Their unique approach of utilizing extensive open-source libraries enabled rapid, cost-effective development while maintaining high-quality, readable code. The engagement continues to evolve, with the platform serving as a proprietary competitive advantage and a potential model for other venture capital firms. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}

Reach-out Message: Hi Eric, Building Safety Team Technologies to simplify OSHA compliance for blue-collar businesses reflects strong domain execution. What helped you translate complex safety workflows into a scalable digital platform? Looking forward to connecting. Parag Google | Founder SoftwareBrio.com
Review Text: Crowdbotics partnered with Safety Team Technologies to design and develop a comprehensive digital platform aimed at simplifying OSHA safety compliance for blue-collar businesses. The project involved building a mobile application, an admin portal, and a user-friendly system that could streamline safety training, recordkeeping, and reporting. The client’s vision was to replace traditional paper-based processes with a modern, automated solution that improves efficiency and reduces compliance risks. Throughout the engagement, Crowdbotics worked on core development as well as continuous feature enhancements and maintenance. The collaboration resulted in a fully functional product that met the client’s primary objective of launching a working platform. However, the journey was not without challenges. The client noted that the process was more complex and time-intensive than initially expected, requiring significant involvement in QA testing, troubleshooting, and refining requirements. There were also some delays due to turnover in project managers and occasional communication barriers, which impacted timelines and coordination. Despite these issues, the client acknowledged the strong technical capabilities of the engineering team and appreciated their efforts to address concerns and improve the product. Crowdbotics demonstrated responsiveness and a willingness to understand the client’s frustrations, ultimately delivering a solution that aligned with the business goals. The post-development support team, in particular, stood out for having an effective project manager who improved the overall experience. The client emphasized the importance of clearly defining all product requirements upfront and being prepared to invest substantial time during development. Overall, the engagement was considered successful, with the delivered platform enabling the client to move forward with their digital safety compliance solution while continuing to expand features with the same development partner.

If you living with unresolved hearing issues such as tinnitus, sensitivity to sound, or diminished hearing, you are not alone.

Most people compensate by using visual cues and the context of a conversation. Others try to block out or ignore unwanted noises that compete for their attention. These strategies may work for the short-term, but they ultimately deplete our energy and cognitive reserve.

As a result, relationships become strained, emotions are more difficult to control, and by the end of the day you have nothing left.

Thankfully, you do not have to live that way. You should be able to live your best life no matter what you have been exposed to. There is hope for you.

All-Inclusive Journey to Costa Rica
At our out-of-this-world eco-lux home, you’ll be immersed in a natural wonderland of creeks, waterfalls, mountains, and jungle. From mandala gardens to stunning sunsets, Michelin-star-caliber meals to the most beautiful temple you’ve seen, your week-long eco-luxury Ayahuasca retreat also includes:

3 Ayahuasca Ceremonies: Led by our extensively trained,massively talented, warm-hearted, and highly relatablemedicine team.
Temazcal (Sweat Lodge) and Ice Baths: Ancient and modern biohacking. This is 1heart in a nutshell.
Immensely Valuable Workshops: Expertly crafted by leading practitioners to amplify the integration of our ceremonial experiences.
Farm-to-Table Cuisine: Locally sourced and made with love, you’ll enjoy delicious meals for breakfast, lunch, dinner, and dessert.
Breathtaking Excursions: Waterfalls, horseback riding, mountain biking, and flower planting bring you intimately in touch with the magic all around you.
Conscious Movement and Practices: Breathwork, yoga, and ecstatic dances drop you into the wisdom of your body, unlike ever before.
Highly Curated Group: Everyone who joins 1heart is interviewed to ensure we are a great fit for one another.
Elevation Groups: Small groups of 6 to 10 guests led by returning alumni for added connection, intimacy, attention, and support.
Impeccable Safety: Creating a safe space for transformation is our top priority and something we do not compromise on.
Play: Our team and workshops will bring out joy, fun, love, and surprises that upgrade everything you ever thought possible.
Living Beyond: A Pioneering 7-Week Virtual Program
Your 1heart experience begins the moment you say yes and doesn’t end after our 1-week Ayahuasca retreat. In fact, the work begins well before and continues after our time in Costa Rica together. 2-Week Pioneering Preparation Program:

3 Zoom calls to connect you with your Tribe, small group, and small group leader, as well as a group chat, dozens of online resources, 1heart-specific exercises, diet-friendly recipes and more to ensure you land in Costa Rica fully prepared for this self-discovery retreat.

8 Zoom calls to land your breakthroughs, create accountability, hold you in your integration process, and provide exercises critical to cementing sustainable life changes.
Mobile App, Portal, and WhatsApp: Valuable digital support, community, and resources to guide you every step of the way.

You cannot find another community in the world like 1heart’s.

Curated Group of Like-Minded Leaders: Journey with amazing humans from all over the world doing inspiring work, all of whom felt a calling to be here.

Review Text: Risingmax Inc partnered with the Kilometers fitness platform, where Shaunie Loretz contributed as an Application Support Member, to enhance and stabilize a GPS-based mobile application used for tracking fitness activities such as walking, running, and calorie burn. The primary objective of the engagement was to resolve critical technical issues affecting the app’s performance, including GPS inaccuracies, pace calculation errors, multipath tracking problems, and application crashes. In addition to improving technical stability, the project also involved upgrading the overall user interface and preparing the platform for broader user adoption through improved usability and design. The scope of work included identifying and fixing core bugs, optimizing GPS tracking accuracy, refining pace and calorie calculations, and conducting extensive testing under different real-world conditions to ensure the application performed reliably across scenarios. Risingmax Inc also implemented UI/UX enhancements, such as updating icons and introducing gesture controls to improve navigation and user engagement. Alongside mobile app improvements, the team worked on web development to support app promotion and expand the platform’s digital presence. The results of the collaboration were highly positive. Indication
Luminopia has been shown to improve visual clarity in amblyopia (lazy eye) patients, aged 4 to <13, associated with vision imbalance and/or with mild crossed eye. Luminopia is intended to be used as an addition to full-time glasses, which should also be worn under the headset during Luminopia treatment. A prescription for Luminopia is required. It should be used at home.
Safety
No serious side effects reported with Luminopia
Most common adverse event reported (treatment vs. control): Headaches (14.3% vs. 1.7%), all graded as mild and transient
Cases of new or worsening eye-turn were balanced between groups (7.1% Luminopia vs. 8.5% control)
Rate of all ocular side effects similar between groups
Safety of Luminopia in children aged 8 - 12 is expected to be in line with the safety in children aged 4 - 7.
Important Information About Luminopia
Patients should only use Luminopia while wearing their prescribed glasses under the headset
Patients should wear Luminopia while seated or lying down.
Patients with a history of light-induced seizures, serious medical conditions, new or worsening eye-turn, or double vision should contact their doctor for additional evaluation and permission
Patients should stop using Luminopia and contact their doctor for additional evaluation if they experience worsening vision in either eye, headache, nausea, eye strain that doesn't go away after usage, dizziness, or increased night terrors
Sources
Luminopia: Directions For Use, LBL-0001 Rev E. Luminopia, Inc; 2025.
Xiao, S., Angjeli, E., Wu, H. C., Gaier, E. D., Gomez, S., Travers, D. A., Binenbaum, G., Langer, R., Hunter, D. G., Repka, M. X., & Luminopia Pivotal Trial Group (2022). Data on file, 2023. Luminopia, Inc.
Study Design
Luminopia was evaluated in a clinical study of 117 patients, ages 4-7 with unilateral amblyopia (lazy eye) associated with anisometropia (vision imbalance) and/or strabismus (crossed eye). The clinical trial compared Luminopia 1 hour/day, 6 days/week with full-time glasses vs full-time glasses alone and measured the change in vision at 12 weeks.

Luminopia was evaluated via a Real-World Registry of 334 patients aged 4 to <13 with a diagnosis of amblyopia (lazy eye) associated with anisometropia (vision imbalance) and/or strabismus (crossed eye). All aspects of treatment including dosage and duration were at the discretion of the prescribing physician. The primary endpoint was change in vision from time of Luminopa prescription to the Last Follow-Up visit available.

Review Text: Savas Labs partnered with the Perinatal Quality Collaborative of North Carolina (PQCNC), where Keith Cochran plays a key role, to design and develop a comprehensive web and database platform supporting statewide healthcare quality improvement initiatives. The primary objective of the project was to create a centralized system that would allow hospitals across North Carolina to input, manage, and analyze data related to perinatal care, ultimately enabling continuous improvement in patient outcomes. The engagement involved building both a public-facing website and a robust backend database using Drupal CMS. The system was designed to support multiple quality initiatives, each requiring customized data collection structures and reporting capabilities. Savas Labs implemented a reporting module that allowed participating hospitals to access their own data, generate insights, and benchmark performance. The platform enabled each hospital to maintain its own account, enter relevant data, and run reports independently, creating a scalable and user-friendly system for distributed healthcare organizations. A key strength of the solution was its balance between technical sophistication and usability. While the underlying system handled complex data management and reporting, it remained accessible to non-technical users, ensuring widespread adoption across participating hospitals. Savas Labs worked closely with PQCNC to understand evolving requirements, regularly delivering tailored solutions for new initiatives based on defined scopes of work. Their ability to translate technical concepts into clear, actionable terms was particularly valuable for stakeholders without deep technical expertise. The results of the engagement were highly impactful. Each initiative supported by the platform enabled hospitals to better understand their processes and improve quality of care. The system became a critical tool for data-driven decision-making, helping healthcare providers identify gaps and implement improvements more effectively. Additionally, Savas Labs demonstrated strong responsiveness by quickly addressing issues, even those outside the original scope, ensuring smooth ongoing operations. Throughout the long-term partnership, Savas Labs exhibited excellent project management and proactive thinking. They consistently met deadlines, adapted to aggressive timelines, and provided forward-looking recommendations that helped the client anticipate future needs. Their collaborative approach, technical depth, and commitment to delivering practical solutions made them a trusted partner in advancing PQCNC’s mission of improving perinatal healthcare outcomes.

We are fostering healthier communities and workplaces through real-time access to community-based providers, nationwide.

Founded by a group of innovative emergency medicine physicians and healthcare entrepreneurs, we’re on a mission to simplify people’s lives by delivering reliable, trusted medical care anytime and anywhere, to ultimately foster healthier communities and workplaces.

It was after hours for the pediatrician and urgent care center, and his sister didn’t know if it required emergency care. Review Text: stable|kernel was engaged by FoodFinderGA, a nonprofit focused on helping individuals locate nearby food assistance programs, to develop a mobile application for both iOS and Android platforms while also improving an existing website’s functionality and responsiveness. The project originated from the founder’s vision to solve food insecurity challenges using geolocation-based technology, enabling users—especially students and families—to easily find nearby food banks and meal services with minimal effort. The engagement began after securing funding through the Arby’s Foundation, and stable|kernel took a highly collaborative and mission-driven approach from the outset. Rather than leading with a sales pitch, their team focused on deeply understanding the problem, even initiating architectural discussions proactively. They first cleaned up the legacy website codebase, significantly improving responsiveness and usability, before transitioning into mobile app development. The team designed the application with a strong emphasis on user experience, ensuring that users could quickly access location-based information such as addresses, directions, and service details with minimal friction. Despite working at cost, stable|kernel demonstrated exceptional commitment, dedicating additional time and resources to ensure the project’s success. Their project management approach was highly structured and communicative, with consistent check-ins, timeline tracking, and responsiveness to evolving needs. The final product met and exceeded expectations, with early metrics indicating growing user adoption and strong engagement, further amplified by local media coverage. The client particularly highlighted the team’s genuine passion for the mission, their thoughtful execution, and their ability to deliver high-quality output without commercial pressure. The collaboration was described as seamless, with no areas for improvement identified, and the experience stood out as significantly better than other mobile development engagements the client had encountered.

FoodFinder is a nationwide 501(c)(3) nonprofit that helps people locate and receive help from their closest food pantry. Our website and app are interactive maps that show when & where people can get food relief nearby for all 50 states. 

FoodFinder is proud to have served more than 600,000 people up to and during the COVID-19 pandemic, and we will continue to strive towards a future where every family in America has the food they need to be happy and healthy.

WebsiteAbout
FoodFinder
Anywhere in America. Any time of year. Everyone should be able to find help.
Hungry families can only get help from places they know about. That’s where we come in.
It seems obvious, but the first step in getting help from any social service program is knowing that it’s out there. FoodFinder exists to bridge that gap – connecting the people looking for emergency food to the programs and places that offer it.


A Different Approach to Fighting Hunger
FoodFinder’s website and app act as food pantry locators that show you when and where to get free food assistance in your area. The interactive map on our home page is a map of food pantries nearby – every red pin you see is a food assistance program nearby. Tap on one to see when they give away food!

A Passion Project Turned Nationwide Nonprofit
Jack Griffin was just a high schooler in 2013 when he tried and failed to find a food pantry to volunteer at near his home in Georgia. He realized that if he was having this much trouble finding a food pantry, what were people who truly need emergency food assistance supposed to do?”


Our service exists for one simple reason: it is unacceptable that food insecure individuals have to jump through so many hoops just to get help for them and their families.

Review Text: Informulate was engaged by Blake Nubar, CEO of Laptop Freedom, to design and develop custom software for a PPC SaaS platform called Rocketeer, aimed at enabling entrepreneurs to efficiently launch and manage paid advertising campaigns. The project scope was extensive, covering end-to-end software development from initial requirements gathering to ongoing support and maintenance. The engagement began with detailed discovery sessions to understand the platform’s objectives, followed by the creation of a comprehensive requirements document outlining both functional and non-functional needs. Informulate then developed a structured project plan with clear timelines, milestones, and resource allocation, ensuring transparency and alignment throughout the process. During the design phase, the team created system architecture diagrams, defined the technology stack, and developed UI/UX prototypes focused on usability and performance. The development phase included full-scale coding, version control management via tools like GitHub, and rigorous testing processes such as unit, integration, and system testing. Informulate also coordinated user acceptance testing to validate the platform’s functionality against business requirements. Deployment was handled with a well-defined rollout strategy, including live environment setup and immediate support to address any issues. Additionally, the team provided comprehensive documentation, training sessions, and user guides to ensure smooth adoption. Post-launch, Informulate continued to offer maintenance and support services under a defined SLA, ensuring system reliability and performance optimization. The client reported that every update functioned perfectly as intended, demonstrating high-quality execution. Informulate’s project management was described as exceptional, with strong attention to detail, timely communication through weekly virtual meetings, and consistent delivery on deadlines. Their problem-solving ability stood out as a key differentiator, along with their responsiveness and structured approach. Overall, the collaboration resulted in a fully functional and reliable SaaS platform, with no areas for improvement identified and strong satisfaction expressed by the client.

Review Text: Informulate was engaged by Employment Technologies Corporation, a leading developer of simulation-based employment tools, to upgrade and modernize their legacy software platform. The project was initiated to address challenges related to outdated code, system architecture, and security, while ensuring that the platform remained reliable and compatible with existing systems. Initially, Informulate conducted a comprehensive review of the platform’s codebase and architecture, delivering actionable insights and recommendations that helped define a clear modernization roadmap. Based on their findings, they proposed a phased upgrade approach that allowed portions of the system to be rewritten and deployed incrementally while maintaining backward compatibility with the existing application. This strategy minimized operational risk and enabled the client to go live with upgraded components without disrupting ongoing services. The engagement involved a small but highly effective team that collaborated closely with the client through regular virtual meetings, messaging platforms, and structured project management tools to track deliverables and progress. Informulate demonstrated a strong agile workflow, enabling iterative development and data-driven decision-making throughout the project lifecycle. Their forward-thinking mindset provided valuable external perspective, helping the client align their platform with modern digital standards and long-term business strategy. As a result of the upgrade, approximately 30% of the client’s customer portfolio provided unsolicited positive feedback, highlighting improved functionality and user experience. Informulate’s responsiveness, attention to detail, and ability to anticipate challenges contributed significantly to the project’s success. The client emphasized that the team consistently delivered on time, communicated proactively about any potential delays, and maintained a high level of transparency. Overall, the collaboration strengthened the client’s confidence in their platform’s future scalability and performance, with no major areas for improvement identified, and the client expressed strong willingness to continue and recommend the partnership.

Sometimes friends can't join you at your favorite watering hole but they want to feel like they're part of the action. Wouldn’t it be great if people could buy each other a round from anywhere in the world? 

Now they can! HADOM is a creative mobile application that lets users buy drinks for their friends at participating bars when they can’t be with them. Using cutting edge transactional technology, the app improves the nightlife experience of users and customers while driving sales for participating bars.

Remote Drink Gifting, Social Gifting, Hospitality Technology, Bar & Restaurant Innovation, Experience Marketing, Guest Engagement Solutions, POS Integration, Mobile Payment Technology, Venue-Based Commerce, Social Commerce, On-premise Beverage Sales, Digital Gifting Platforms, Loyalty & Customer Retention, Alcohol Beverage Technology, Enterprise Hospitality Solutions, Location-Based Services, Consumer Mobile Apps, Hospitality Revenue Optimization, and Branded Promotions & Activations
About HADOM
What is HADOM?
HADOM is a patented mobile app that lets friends share the fun of buying rounds, even when they can’t be together.

​

Here’s how it works: when someone checks in at a participating venue, their friends can use HADOM to send them a drink — or any menu item the venue chooses to make available — from anywhere in the world. The recipient gets a notification and redeems it right at the bar.

​

Why people love it:​

​

It keeps friends connected through real-time social gifting.

It makes nights out more interactive and fun.

It works seamlessly with venues — no awkward IOUs or missed rounds.

 

Why venues love it:

​

Incremental sales from customers who aren’t even in the room.

No commissions — you keep the full value of every sale.

Flexibility — HADOM can feature drinks, desserts, dinners, or coffee, depending on your menu.

Built-in compliance — all alcohol service happens in-venue with staff checking ID, just like any other order.

 

Our Story

​

 

Founded by bar enthusiasts who never want to miss out on the fun, HADOM is about connection, celebration, and keeping the spirit of hospitality alive — no matter the distance.

​

​

HADOM founders
glenn_about.jpg
Chief Executive Officer

Glenn Herdling

HADOM was the brainchild of Glenn Herdling, who worked in the mobile application business at Verizon Wireless. Prior to that, Glenn held various marketing and creative positions in the healthcare and financial sector as well as at Marvel Comics. 

​

A bar patron for 30 years, Glenn has an in-depth knowledge of the needs of bar-goers.

It was apparent to him that an unserved market need existed for the HADOM app. While he has never run a mobile application himself, he was responsible for the development of various mobile services, products, and mobile applications including Verizon’s LG children’s devices and the launch of its 5G network. As such, Glenn has a well-rounded knowledge of the business, including the operations side (e.g., running day-to-day operations) and the business management side (e.g., staffing, marketing, etc.). 

about_brian.jpg
Chief Creative Officer

Brian Dusman

Brian has over 25 solid years of creative design and user experience. Coming from a background of traditional and digital art and design, along with a degree in Computer Science, Brian has had many successful years as a Creative Director with several top Fortune 500 companies.

 

During the day Brian currently maps out the experience and designs for mobile applications and smart watches. He has an amazing eye for detail and knows how to convey the right message to consumers through the art of simplistic design. Brian was key in defining the HADOM brand and design, and creating the user experience for consumers and businesses for mobile, tablet and desktop. 

neha_about.jpg
Chief Product Officer

Neha Mohta

Neha has had a corporate career spanning over 12 years in multiple roles including marketing, finance, and operations. She has been fortunate to work with large multi-national companies like The Linde Group (Calcutta, India) and Western Union (Colorado, USA) in global roles working with teams all over the world. She has led projects in multiple countries, such as Thailand, Romania, and Democratic Republic of Congo. She brings a very pragmatic outlook to everything she does and strongly believes in execution. Neha also co-founded PurpleSlate where she strived to create a delightful user experience with the help of text-based technology for people who want to get together in the real world. She is well connected in the local startup community in Denver and consults with various local startups. 

about_ravi2.jpg
Chief Technology Officer

Ravi Bhalotia

Ravi is a seasoned technology executive with over 15 years of experience in implementing mobile/web technologies, working with large corporations as well as multiple startups. He has been associated with two startups and co-founded two other startups and has had one successful exit. One of his startups was Sweet Relish, where he launched a mobile platform that sourced coupons from majo r brands such as General Mills, P&G and distributed them through a REST-based API to mobile app developers with a goal to convert paper grocery coupons into a digital platform with traceable KPIs. Recently he co-founded PurpleSlate, a mobile app that sends event invites and collects RSVP via text messages in seconds. He played a critical role in transforming the product based on user feedback. He is also an advisor/consultant to multiple local startups in Denver, CO. 

ernie_about.jpg
Chief Financial Officer

Ernest Villany

For over thirty years, Ernie has worked with hundreds of individuals and small to medium-sized businesses. He has formed countless rewarding business relationships locally and globally and has helped various start-up clients grow and succeed.

HADOM history
On March 9, 2019, Glenn Herdling was skiing with his two sons when he hit a patch of ice and landed on his shoulder . . . hard. He knew that he had dislocated it, but he skied the rest of the way down the mountain and walked into the first aid office. The professionals there popped his shoulder back into place and strongly suggested that he go to the hospital.

 

But Glenn didn’t want to ruin the ski trip for his boys, so he sent them back out onto the slopes and nursed his wounds at the bar in the lodge. He posted his predicament on social media and asked, “What would you do?” Most of his friends and contacts applauded his decision to remain in the bar. One of his friends remarked, “I’m having a drink for you.” 

 

Glenn was about to reply in jest, “Why not send a drink to me?” when he was struck with inspiration. Does such a system exist? 

 

After surveying publishers of mobile applications, bar-goers, bar owners, bartenders, and sommeliers, Glenn determined that an app with this capability does not exist. So, he established Hadom Enterprises and went straight to work on creating one. The rest, as they say, is history.

linkedin company about:
Country Natural Beef is about sustainability. Doc and Connie Hatfield started the co-op in 1986 with the goal of bridging the gap between rural ranchers and urban consumers. In 1986 they created this statement:

Our Goal is to provide a sustainable means through a group to profitably market quality beef products desired by the consumer while maintaining every possible bit of independence. Our plan is to return proceeds realized from marketing Country Natural Beef to the rancher‚ rather than for the organization to acquire capital assets. Guidelines for the organization are: to be grass roots producer controlled ‚ to contain a bare minimum of administrative costs, and for the costs of operation to come from a percentage of producer’s revenue. Country Natural Beef is an idea to be constantly examined, not an entity that can be bought or sold.

Today there are roughly 90 member ranches in the co-op and we continually strive to produce a product that is desired by consumers, and sell it for a price that allows ranchers to stay in business for decades to come.

LEARN ABOUT OUR BEEF
We’re a network of forward-thinking ranchers united by a common philosophy: Leave the land better than you found it.
Over the past 36 years, Country Natural Beef has grown to become the largest ranching cooperative in the western US. Our 100 members — and counting — in 9 states represent the best of the West. Over the years, we’ve honed practices that improve soil, protect streams, draw more carbon dioxide out of the air, and raise healthy, happy cows across 6.5 million acres. By managing every part of the system synergistically — soil, water, plants and animals — we work with Mother Nature rather than against her. You can see the difference on the landscape, and you can taste the difference on your plate.

A lot has changed since 1986. Rangelands are drier, water is scarcer, and the climate is changing fast. Caring for the land, air and water must become the norm rather than the exception, and that’s what we’re doing with regenerative ranching. The future of our communities, our food systems and rural economies — here in the West and around the world — depend on a new way of operating, and that’s Grazewell.

Whether you raise beef, eat it or sell it, we welcome you to the Country Natural Beef community. Together, we can create a better, more sustainable future for the land, for cattle and for ourselves.

The mission of MAKE WAY FOR BOOKS (MWFB) is to give all children the chance to read and succeed.. MWFB provides a full continuum of early literacy education, programs, and resources to make sharing books an important and enjoyable part of families'​ lives. Our highest priority is to serve economically disadvantaged families with young children (birth through five) and early childhood professionals in under-served areas of Tucson and southern Arizona. MWFB envisions a community in which all children enter kindergarten with the necessary early literacy skills to be successful in school and throughout life.

The Importance of Early Literacy
Success begins way before a child ever steps foot in a classroom. If we make sure young children experience quality early education and literacy-rich environments, if we ensure they have developmentally appropriate books and adults who read to them every day, they have a better chance of getting to kindergarten ready to learn. When children start kindergarten prepared, they are far more likely to graduate from high school, stay out of trouble with the law, and become productive members of the workforce.Early Literacy, Infants and Toddlers, Family Literacy, and Books

About us
Our mission is to give all children the chance to read and succeed.
Our Impact
Make Way for Books is an early literacy 501(c)(3) nonprofit that provides proven programs, services, and resources to more than 30,000 young children, parents, and educators throughout southern Arizona each year. Our mission is to give all children the chance to read and succeed. We provide proven programs to ensure our youngest children discover the joy of books, develop critical emergent literacy and language skills, and start school ready. We reach young children in the critical birth to 5 years to build a foundation for all future learning. Our theory of change is that we get to every caregiver in a child’s life and ensure that, that adult understands their incredible power to impact their child’s literacy, love of learning, and success.

In 2022, we received the Library of Congress American Prize, which is awarded for significant and measurable contribution to increasing literacy levels in the United States.



A snapshot of impact across our programs:
97% of children develop critical emergent literacy and language skills that are the building blocks for future successful readers

98% of parents/caregivers gain skills and confidence to be their child’s first, most essential teacher

99% of early childhood educators gain skills to support the children in their care more effectively

Based on a sample of participating children, 75% of children who participated in Make Way for Books programming during their Pre-K years went on to score at or above benchmarks on standardized reading assessments given in kindergarten, first, second, and third grades

—
Our Approach
We recognize literacy is a foundation for social justice – it is a dynamic, transformative, and empowering process which can lead to personal and wider social change. As an early literacy organization, Make Way for Books works to create further educational equity and to change the educational and literacy landscape by ensuring more under-served children have the opportunity to discover the joy and power of books and reading. We exist to create social change in our community via education and a two-generation method of change that builds the agency of and connections between our families and caregivers. Empowerment is the foundation for all of our work. Empowerment is about breaking down barriers, building on strengths, and creating opportunities from those strengths. It is that positive force that carries hope and opportunity and creates genuine possibility and results.

Make Way for Books honors and celebrates the many perspectives, languages, cultures, and funds of knowledge that children and families possess. We work to ensure our staff reflects the children and families we serve and we build on the diverse strengths of families in our community to create lasting change for our youngest children.

 



—
Our story
When our founder Dr. Mary Jan Bancroft volunteered to read in Tucson classrooms, she found that many preschools and childcare centers serving economically-disadvantaged children in Tucson did not have quality books and literacy resources. To meet the needs she saw, Mary Jan founded Make Way for Books in 1998. With the support of six volunteers, Make Way for Books began providing books and literacy workshops at five under-resourced preschools. In the decades since, we have grown to serve children, educators, and families at hundreds of sites across the state.

 



 

—
Our Core Values
We have core values by which we do all of our work.

 



—
Where we’re going
Make Way for Books is committed to innovation. We work strategically and creatively to provide programs and strategies that meet the diverse needs of young children, families, and educators. Click here to see our 2022-27 Strategic Plan.

Like all origin stories, Sidekick's is personal
Sarah Shaw Pic_edited.jpg
Meet Sarah!
When I was young, I knew I wanted to go into Medicine. I did everything I could to make that dream a reality – taking math and science classes, doing job shadows, and watching re-runs of Rescue 911. I was on a clear path until my junior year of high school when I met with my school counselor about enrolling in a Physics class. My counselor told me, “No girl in our school has ever passed this class.” I took this to mean I couldn’t be successful in a class like Physics, and, by extension, other higher-level math and science courses. I let go of my dream of medicine, and by the time I went to college, I chose a degree as far from medicine as possible.

I wanted to make sure every young person could tell a different story. That’s why I started Sidekick. Our personal champion is always there to show students just what they’re capable of and connect them to meaningful relationships with people who will help them achieve it. So the next time a young person hears what she can’t do, she can point to all the people and evidence showing exactly what she can.

It wasn’t until my second job after college when I had to learn to code that I realized I wasn’t actually bad at math or computational thinking. I realized just how much that conversation, when I was 16, had shaped my understanding of my own abilities and potential. I also learned my story isn’t unique. Students everywhere, even today, have to overcome expectations that are often unintentential but always undeserved All of those undeserved expectations lead to unrealized potential in our young people and in the communities where they live.

PracticePlan allows facilities of all types to access a competitive marketplace with increased exposure and marketing. The PracticePlan platform allows sports facilities and other venues to be seen by area users, allowing the users to make practice venue decisions based on proximity, price, and availability.  
 
For consumers, finding fields, courts, and other practice locations for coaches, teams, athletes, and parents of athletes, can be a very time consuming and manual process, leaving many available options off the table simply due to a lack of information or exposure. PracticePlan’s platform will streamline the process, giving users visibility into all available options in their area, even some they may not have known existed.

If users do not have information or knowledge of a facility due to lack of marketing exposure, they will now, thanks to PracticePlan. Venue owners will also benefit from the easy to use, streamlined process, freeing up valuable employee time to focus on other things.

Review Text: They had a really smooth delivery with expected results. As the Head of Technology Business for a Boston-based growth company focused on helping startups and scale-ups succeed in the US market, I was responsible for overseeing a technically complex project that had previously stalled under two different IT providers. The platform, originally developed for a government-related initiative involving Building Energy and the Department of Energy, required significant effort to spin back up, stabilize, and update before it could be demonstrated to potential investors and pilot partners. The core technology stack involved Django, Angular, and PostgreSQL, and the key challenge was not just technical recovery but ensuring the system was functional and presentable within a constrained timeline. After unsuccessful attempts with prior vendors, we engaged DTEAM, who immediately stood out for their structured and thoughtful approach. Rather than rushing into execution, they took the time to fully understand the problem, assess the existing codebase, and align expectations before committing to a solution. This initial diligence set the tone for the entire engagement. Once onboarded, they were given access to the GitHub repository and began working closely with our internal product manager, while maintaining clear communication through regular meetings and progress tracking. Their workflow was particularly effective given our limited bandwidth to manage day-to-day technical details. DTEAM took ownership of tracking hours, managing deliverables, and proactively communicating any blockers, ensuring that issues were addressed quickly and did not disrupt progress. The team composition was lean but effective, consisting of a project manager and two developers on their side, which allowed for both agility and accountability. From a results perspective, DTEAM successfully brought the platform back online, updated it, and ensured it was stable enough to be demonstrated to investors and stakeholders. This was a critical milestone, as it enabled us to move forward with pilot discussions and continue development in a new strategic direction. In fact, the initial version of the updated solution contributed to generating over $100,000 in sales, highlighting the tangible business impact of their work. What impressed me most was their organization, technical knowledge, and ability to work within an agile framework. They demonstrated strong expertise in both Angular and Django, and were flexible enough to adapt to changing requirements without compromising quality or timelines. Their delivery was smooth, predictable, and aligned with expectations throughout the project lifecycle. While their services were not the cheapest, the value they delivered justified the investment, particularly given the complexity and urgency of the project. Overall, DTEAM proved to be a highly reliable and capable partner, successfully turning around a challenging situation and enabling us to regain momentum on a critical initiative.

Reach-out Message: Hi David, Building Chamba to connect employers with Hispanic job seekers through a culturally aligned mobile experience reflects strong product-market understanding. What helped you translate user empathy into a platform that resonated across diverse age groups? Looking forward to connecting. Parag Google | Founder SoftwareBrio.com
Review Text: We felt we were in safe hands, that the product would be better crafted and more complete. As the co-founder of Chamba, a platform designed to connect employers with Hispanic job seekers, I was responsible for driving the vision behind a culturally relevant and highly functional mobile application. Our primary challenge was building a product across both iOS and Android platforms without having in-house technical expertise. We needed a development partner who could not only execute but also guide us strategically in shaping the product. After evaluating several agencies, we chose EIGHT25MOBILE because they immediately demonstrated a deeper level of engagement with our idea. From the very first interaction, they asked thoughtful and logical questions that challenged our assumptions and improved the direction of the product. Their approach went beyond simply building what we requested—they acted as a strategic partner, almost like an embedded CTO and product manager, offering insights, resources, and recommendations that elevated the overall quality of the application. The project was executed in two main phases. The first phase involved designing and building the core application, including both the user-facing mobile apps in English and Spanish and the backend admin platform. The second phase focused on information storage and matching logic, enabling employers to connect with candidates based on skills, availability, and location. The app included a questionnaire-style profile system that functioned as a simplified resume, making it easier for users to engage with the platform. During development, we realized that simply translating the app into Spanish was not sufficient to resonate with our target audience. This led to a redesign effort, where we worked with another company to refine the cultural alignment of the product while continuing development with EIGHT25MOBILE. This collaborative approach ultimately resulted in a product that felt authentic and intuitive to users. From a results standpoint, the application was highly successful in terms of user adoption and engagement. Hispanic workers across a wide demographic—from older individuals to younger users—found the app easy to use and appreciated that it was designed specifically for them. This level of cultural alignment is often overlooked in similar platforms, but it proved to be a key differentiator for us. EIGHT25MOBILE’s project management was effective, especially once we became familiar with their tools and processes, such as Basecamp. They were responsive, action-oriented, and consistently focused on delivering value. One of the most impressive aspects of their work was their willingness to suggest cost-saving measures and long-term architectural decisions that would benefit us beyond the initial launch. Overall, EIGHT25MOBILE proved to be a highly capable and trustworthy partner, delivering a product that not only met but exceeded our expectations, while also providing strategic guidance that was critical to the success of the platform.
