K12 Lift aims to increase equity and joy in public education by helping principals and teachers learn from student academic outcomes and one another.

We want to be part of your story.

Skip to content

FAQ
Blog
Contact Us

We’ve lost the thread.
I’ve come to believe one of the reasons we’ve seen so little progress in improving our public schools over the last several decades is that we can’t seem to agree on what we’re intending to make better.

Parental involvement? Student wellness? Standardized test scores? Attendance? Our football teams?

At K12 Lift, we’re about helping our partners do one thing: improve the quality of their Tier 1 instruction.

To us, improving schools means improving academic outcomes, and improving outcomes requires improving the quality of instruction students receive on the regular. Before interventions. Before afterschool tutoring. Before parents help with homework.

If this worldview resonates…

Keep reading
Cut to the chase

I’m Sam. I’m not a data guy.
I am an engineer.

I’m curious. I enjoy wrestling with a good problem and building cool looking stuff. I believe in making things better, especially for other people.

I came to public education as a second career – first as an associate superintendent in a school district and later as a deputy chancellor at the Florida Department of Education.

In those roles, I desperately wanted clarity around what was working and what wasn’t. We were surrounded by data… but had few insights useful in helping us improve.

That was frustrating. State education agencies (SEAs) had worked hard to provide good academic standards and good assessments.

What they were missing was great reporting.

So, I created  

Keep reading
Cut to the chase
Building Alignment Around a Common Goal
I designed data visualizations that, with training, allow principals, academic coaches, teachers, and administrators to answer questions they could never get at before:

Is our Tier 1 instruction effective? For whom?
Which content are we teaching well (or not well enough)?
Who’s already getting great results with students that look like mine?

With answers to these questions, they can design teacher-facing professional learning experiences that speak directly to the “why?” and “what now?” of their current outcomes.

After those trainings have been conducted and teachers have been given an opportunity to experiment with the new strategies they’ve learned, our next round of reports can be used to determine if the changes in instructional practice have had the desired effect.

Importantly, our reports use progress monitoring and state assessment data they already have – so no new testing.


Here’s what we offer…
Reports you can’t unsee
meaningfully contextualized, print-ready, easy to read, and fun to look at

Training you’ll look forward to
learners doing the talking, confronting misconceptions, constructing meaning, and kindling curiosity

Our individualized portfolios and trainings can be the basis of transformational change – a movement away from traditional accountability and public shaming and toward aspirational teaching.

A movement away from abstract notions of “school improvement” and toward concrete, specific bodies of work that prepare teachers to have routine, productive, joyful interactions with students around appropriately challenging content.

If you’re nodding your head “Yes!”…

Keep reading
Let me show you
Or, I could tell you.
Over the years we’ve seen it all.
And I have some really inspiring short stories to share…

A veteran superintendent knew what she wanted to do in a new district but didn’t have the information she needed to do it.

A team of instructional coaches who had content knowledge and teacher trust but suspected the data was misleading.

A district leadership team who wanted each of their principals to speak about academic success using the same language.

We’re a unicorn. Seriously.
I know every company likes to think they’re unique. Here are a few ways we actually are different than any other data reporting company in the public education space:

We change how our partners think and talk about academic performance data
Our reporting is aimed directly at, and only at, improving Tier 1 instruction
We provide our visualizations in print-ready, user-specific PDF portfolios with embedded paths of inquiry. No online dashboards.
Our trainers are true data experts with deep experience confronting uncomfortable truths with teachers, principals, district administrators, and state education agency specialists
Our training model is designed to build internal training capacity from day 1. Think of it like train-the-trainer meets gradual release.
The professionals behind the curtain.
Building K12 Lift hasn’t been easy, in part because making our stuff look and sound simple is incredibly complicated.

Here are the people making it happen:










Shannon Houston
Official title: Chief Learning Facilitator

Unofficial title: Air Traffic Controller

In any given day: Curating meaningful adult learning experiences; wrangling the whirlwind of who, what, when and why

Strange talent: Finding the best local food spots


















Asking educators to set aside what they think they know, and embrace a different truth in service of the kids they teach, is really hard.

We’re great at that part too, and that’s what makes us different.

Let us prove it to you

Driven by the community, for the community, Colorado Startup Week unites the state’s entrepreneurial ecosystem, connects a global network of like-minded innovators, and celebrates the people, companies, and ideas shaping what’s next. With hundreds of sessions spanning topics from robotics to fundraising, CSW offers something for everyone who believes in building the future.

At Colorado Startup Week, we are committed to creating an inclusive space where all attendees can connect, learn, and grow—regardless of gender identity, gender expression, race, ability, sexual orientation, or any combination of those identities.

A live pitch competition spotlighting rising startups from across the state, giving founders a platform to share bold ideas with investors.

Founder panels focused on fundraising, scaling, and building resilient teams.

Community-building events such as evening mixers, rooftop happy hours, and neighborhood meetups connecting attendees from every corner of Colorado’s startup ecosystem.

Get to Know Colorado
Startup Week
Colorado Startup Week is a celebration of everything entrepreneurial in Colorado and the largest free event of its kind. Since 2012, our annual event routinely brings over 10,000 people together with 200+ programs and events to celebrate Colorado’s thriving entrepreneurial ecosystem and bring the community together in the center city’s culture of innovation.

The weeklong event is intended to unite the entrepreneurial community in Colorado and beyond, and celebrate great companies, innovation, ideas, and people. Events throughout the week are organized by the community and our volunteers. It includes sessions, presentations, panels, workshops, happy hours, celebratory events, job fairs and more.

The depth and functionality of both sides of the platform significantly enhanced the overall value proposition of our offering. Overall, RedSky Engineering exceeded expectations in every aspect of the project, delivering a high-quality, feature-rich platform while maintaining a strong focus on collaboration, innovation, and client success.

Reach-out Message: Hi Robert, Leading Kinetic Ceramics in developing advanced piezoelectric systems reflects strong execution in highly specialized engineering domains. What helped you translate complex hardware innovation into scalable, reliable system architecture? Looking forward to connecting. Parag Google | Founder SoftwareBrio.com
Review Text: We essentially had to invent new systems together, and they delivered on that. From a technical and product development standpoint, the collaboration with AJProTech was fundamental in advancing the capabilities of our high-tech pump systems at Kinetic Ceramics. As a research-driven manufacturing company focused on piezoelectric technologies, we operate in a space where standard electronic solutions simply do not exist, requiring custom-built systems that push the boundaries of both hardware and software engineering. The primary objective of this engagement was to modernize our legacy electronics by transitioning from a large, cumbersome system to a more compact, efficient, and intelligent solution. This involved developing advanced control logic that could process real-time inputs from pressure and temperature sensors embedded within our pumps and dynamically adjust voltage frequency to optimize performance. AJProTech was tasked with not only creating this control logic but also engineering the supporting high-voltage electronics required to drive our systems. Their work included the development of a 15,000-volt power supply with an adjustable amplifier, as well as integrating wireless connectivity between the control logic and the hardware components. This level of innovation required a deep understanding of both high-voltage electronics and IoT-based control systems, and AJProTech demonstrated strong expertise in both areas. The project evolved over several years, with continuous iterations and improvements based on testing, feedback, and changing requirements. One of the most significant strengths of AJProTech was their ability to adapt and improve over time. While there were occasional fluctuations in timelines and communication, particularly during periods of rapid team expansion and external challenges, their overall trajectory showed clear growth in both performance and project management capabilities. In the later stages of the project, their delivery timelines became more accurate, and communication improved significantly, resulting in a more streamlined and efficient collaboration. From a results perspective, the quality of their work was consistently high, and they were able to meet the technical specifications outlined at the beginning of the project. Their willingness to engage deeply with the problem, accept feedback, and iterate on solutions was critical to achieving the desired outcomes. The ability to co-develop entirely new systems—something that only a limited number of teams are capable of—was a defining aspect of this partnership. What stood out most was their capability to handle highly complex engineering challenges and deliver functional, high-quality solutions. Their expertise, combined with a collaborative approach, made them a valuable partner in advancing our technology. Overall, despite some areas for improvement in communication consistency, the experience was highly positive, and AJProTech proved to be a capable and reliable partner in developing innovative, next-generation systems. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}
linkedin company about:
I'm the founder of Nav, a personal AI that runs a 400-billion-parameter frontier model entirely on a phone.
 
Most of the industry is scaling AI by scaling the cloud. I think the architecture is backwards. The personal AI category (finance, email, calendar, the things we actually want to DO with AI) can't be served by sending that data to someone else's data center. The trust problem is structural, not a policy issue, and it won't be solved by better terms of service.
 
So I built the alternative. Nav runs on Aether, a runtime that decomposes frontier-scale Mixture-of-Experts models for streaming inference on consumer hardware. Today, a 397B-parameter model runs coherently on an iPhone Air at 1.5 tokens/second within an 8GB Metal memory budget.  Our 35B model runs at up to 9 tokens/second with full complex reasoning and tool calling.  Completely on-device. The same techniques extend up the hardware curve: we're working on running 1T+ parameter models on a single GH200, with the goal of making frontier on-prem deployment economically viable for enterprises that can't or won't use cloud inference.
 
The broader thesis: the future of AI isn't a handful of hyperscale clusters. It's billions of sovereign models on phones, in vehicles, on private servers: AI specialized to local context, coordinating with each other under governance built into the language of the models themselves. Knowledge externalized to data. Capability internalized to small, fast, streaming-optimized models. Trust as an architectural property, not a compliance checkbox.
 
Nav is the first product in that future. Aether is the engine. The governance stack with visible boundaries, verification before action, earned autonomy, full reversibility ships with every install.
 
If you're working on edge inference, MoE architectures, distributed AI governance, or sovereign-AI deployment for enterprises, I'd like to talk. If you want to see a 400B model run on a phone, check out our X account. @nav_app

my story
Online since 1980. Still Building. Still Pioneering. Still Leading.
Over 45 years in tech. From my first computer in 1980 to building one of the first websites in 1995 to AI today — I’ve been leading and pioneering in the arena for every technological shift, and I’m still standing.


I got my first computer in 1980 — a TRS-80 Model 1 with 4K of RAM — and taught myself basic code and how to use a computer before most people had heard the word “modem.” By 1995 I’d built one of the first 18,000 websites in the world. Every new wave of technology felt like a puzzle I had to solve.

Some of those puzzles turned into bestselling books. Others turned into businesses, podcasts, products, and stages around the world. The thread through all of it? I show up early, I learn by doing, and I share what I find. That’s been the formula since day one.

the timeline
Four Decades of Building
1980

First Computer
Got my hands on a TRS-80 and taught myself how to use it. No YouTube tutorials. No Instagram. Just a manual, curiosity and a passion for the technology. This is where it all began.

1995

One of the First 18,000 Websites
Built WorldVillage.com when the entire web was smaller than a small town. The internet felt like a frontier, and I wanted to homestead. I knew this was the future.

1998

ClassicGames.com
Turned a passion for games into one of the top gaming destinations on the web. Millions of visitors, long before “content creator” was a job title.

2006

NY Times Bestseller
Published The AdSense Code and hit the New York Times bestseller list. It became the playbook for a generation of online publishers figuring out how to actually make money on the web.

2006-current

16 Books Published
Wrote 15 more books covering digital business, social media, Twitter, mobile apps, and live video. Each one tackled the platform or trend that was reshaping how people connected and did business.

2015

TEDx Speaker
Took the TEDxMileHigh stage to talk about something that still drives everything I do: being human in a digital world. Technology changes. People don’t.

2017

Bad Crypto Podcast Launches
Co-founded what became one of the most popular crypto podcasts in the world. Now in its 9th year with more than 10 million downloads, still going strong, still making blockchain make sense.

2018-2022

Blockchain, NFTs, Web3
Went deep into the decentralized web. Minted over 1.5 million NFTs. Built, collected, invested, and helped thousands of people understand what was real and what was hype in the crypto space.

2023+

AI Keynote Speaker & Podcast Host
what drives me
Curiosity. Pattern Recognition.
Sharing What I Find.
I’ve built an entire career out of testing technologies, finding what works and sharing my results. My books, podcasts, webinar, keynotes, workshops and videos have helped millions of people.

01
Curiosity That Won’t Quit
I’ve never been able to look at a new technology and not wonder what it could do. That curiosity is what got me online in 1995, into crypto in 2017, and into AI before most people had heard of ChatGPT. I don’t chase trends. I follow genuine fascination.

02
Seeing Patterns Early
When you’ve lived through the birth of the web, the rise of social media, the mobile revolution, the blockchain wave, and the AI explosion, you start to see the patterns. New tech follows familiar arcs. I help people recognize where we are in the cycle so they can move with confidence.

03
Helping Others See What’s Next
The best part of being early isn’t bragging rights. It’s being able to turn around and say, “Hey, come look at this.” Whether it’s through a book, a podcast, or a keynote, my goal is always the same: make the complex simple and help people take action.

What is TurnSignl?
TurnSignl is an on-demand, real-time mobile app that provides 24/7 legal guidance from an attorney over video chat — all while recording the interaction. When users are stopped by law enforcement officers or involved in a car accident, they can access this live service from their phone at the press of a button. TurnSignl attorneys are vetted and trained to de-escalate interactions between police, drivers, and passengers.

The TurnSignl platform was born out of necessity, in the same city where George Floyd tragically lost his life to police violence.

Download. Get home safe.


Our History
2016       Philando Castile was killed by law enforcement.

2020      History repeated itself, and George Floyd lost his life at the hands of law enforcement in our home state of Minnesota. In October, our Co-Founders left their corporate careers and began development of the TurnSignl app.

2021 - 2023        The TurnSignl app launched in Minnesota and expanded to Georgia, California, New York, and eventually, all states across the country!

2024 - 2025       TurnSignl became a must-have for leading workplace benefits programs, available in employee benefit and group legal plans for over 150 companies nationwide.

2026       TurnSignl launched key new features, including support for 60+ languages and support during immigration matters.

Where is TurnSignl Available?

Download Today
Our Leadership Team


Jazz Hampton, Esq.
CEO & General Counsel


Mychal Frelix, MBA
Chief Operating Officer


Cree Larson
Sr. Director of Growth & Marketing

What makes TurnSignl different?
High-Quality Legal Guidance
TurnSignl provides 24/7/365 access to legal guidance from attorneys who are experts at keeping drivers safe and protected. Attorneys are trained to de-escalate potentially volatile encounters and advocate for users’ civil rights during traffic stops, car accidents, immigration matters, and other roadside interactions. 

You have the RIGHT to get home safe.

Advanced Technology
The TurnSignl app is easy to use. Signing up takes one minute, and the user experience is intuitive. In the event of a traffic-related incident, drivers can press a button or use a voice command to connect with an attorney and record the event. All interactions are stored and encrypted into the user’s personal cloud.

You have the RIGHT to feel safe in your own skin.

Commitment to Social Justice
TurnSignl is proud to be a Black-owned, mission-driven company, and our team is dedicated to the advancement of social justice and equity for all. We value hard work, optimism, innovation, and passion in everything we do. 

TurnSignl was created to help all drivers, with a particular focus on non-white and LGBTQ+ individuals.

Your RIGHTS. Right at hand.

Reach-out Message: Hi Dena, Building Running Mate into a real-time safety platform for runners reflects strong execution and purpose-driven leadership. What helped you translate personal experiences into a scalable and reliable product for users? Looking forward to connecting. Parag Google | Founder SoftwareBrio.com
Review Text: We engaged The Jed Mahonis Group to design and develop our cross-platform mobile application, Running Mate, which is focused on providing a safe and personalized experience for runners by connecting them with verified running partners in real time. From the beginning, the engagement was highly collaborative, starting with detailed brainstorming sessions to ensure the team fully understood our vision and long-term goals. They have been instrumental in translating that vision into a functional and scalable product, offering valuable insights and suggestions throughout the process that enhanced the overall design and usability of the application. The platform is being built for both iOS and Android and includes a dual-interface system consisting of a runner side and a mate side. On the mate side, users can create detailed profiles that include information such as running experience, certifications, and preferences. On the runner side, users can create accounts, store payment details, and use geolocation to find available running partners nearby. One of the most impressive features is the integration of AI technology to match runners with compatible mates based on preferences and requirements, ensuring a more tailored and safe experience. Additional features include run tracking, sharing live location with trusted contacts, customizable run settings such as communication preferences, and post-run reviews and tipping functionality. The team operates on a structured monthly development plan and has consistently delivered ahead of schedule, completing major components like the mate profile early and progressing efficiently on the runner interface. Communication has been seamless through regular Zoom calls, Slack, and Trello, allowing for transparent updates and quick resolution of any issues. Their responsiveness to bugs and feedback has been exceptional, with immediate action taken whenever needed. What stands out most is their ability to take abstract ideas and transform them into a tangible product that aligns exactly with our expectations while staying within budget. Their professionalism, attentiveness, and technical expertise have made this partnership highly productive. Overall, I am extremely satisfied with their performance and the progress made so far, and I would highly recommend them to anyone seeking a reliable and skilled development partner.

Reach-out Message: Hi Mike, Building iNSUPPLi as a data-driven platform for pricing and delivery insights reflects strong execution in a complex domain. What helped you build trust in outsourced development after facing challenges with previous teams? Looking forward to connecting. Parag Google | Founder SoftwareBrio.com
Review Text: We engaged The Jed Mahonis Group to design and develop our mobile application for iNSUPPLi after facing repeated challenges with previous outsourced development teams, particularly around communication, scope clarity, and execution reliability. From the beginning, their team demonstrated a strong ability to understand our requirements and translate them into a structured and executable development plan. The project involved full-stack mobile application development for both iOS and Android platforms, including front-end user experience and backend infrastructure. The application is designed as a price and delivery data platform, and at the time of evaluation, it had reached its final testing phases with The Jed Mahonis Group continuing to provide support and maintenance services. Throughout the engagement, their team, which included senior leadership and experienced developers, consistently met deadlines and achieved key milestones, something we had struggled with when working with other vendors. Their communication has been clear, proactive, and highly reliable, utilizing tools such as Jira, Slack, email, and weekly Zoom meetings to ensure alignment and quick resolution of any issues. One of the most valuable aspects of working with them has been their ability to simplify complex technical discussions, allowing us to make informed decisions without needing deep technical expertise. They not only followed our scope of work but also actively contributed solutions and improvements based on their experience, which significantly enhanced the overall quality of the product. Their responsiveness to challenges and ability to overcome obstacles that previous vendors could not handle gave us a high level of confidence in their capabilities. As a result, we have developed a strong level of trust in their work and plan to continue the partnership for future feature development. While their services may come at a slightly higher cost compared to offshore options, the quality, reliability, and professionalism they bring to the table make the investment worthwhile. Overall, we are extremely satisfied with their performance and would highly recommend them to anyone seeking a dependable and communicative development partner.

Reach-out Message: Hi Phil, Building VSI Labs into a research platform for ADAS and automated driving reflects deep technical focus and long-term industry execution. What helped you turn complex automotive research into a device-independent client experience? Looking forward to connecting. Parag Google | Founder SoftwareBrio.com
Review Text: We engaged The Jed Mahonis Group to develop and maintain a cloud-based research platform for VSI Labs, allowing our clients to access and consume automotive research assets in a device-independent environment. Our need went beyond a standard website because the platform had to support the delivery of research, advisory content, and client-facing digital services for companies working in active safety and automated driving. The Jed Mahonis Group handled the design, maintenance, and backend development of the platform, including databases, API calls, and supporting infrastructure, while also working with third-party hosting tools such as AWS and project staging through Trello. From the beginning, their team provided a hands-on and personable working experience, combining technical execution with creativity and flexibility. They were able to help shape new ideas, translate them into workable product improvements, and remain open to trying new approaches whenever the platform required enhancements. Their communication has been strong, transparent, and consistent, and they have been reliable in setting expectations and sticking to agreed timelines. What stood out most was their diligence and creativity, especially when we needed help formulating ideas into functional platform capabilities. They have continued to support the platform since the initial engagement began in October 2014, helping us launch our first product within six to eight months and supporting ongoing enhancements since then. Overall, their work has been a strong fit for our needs, and their ability to combine technical capability with clear communication has made the long-term partnership highly valuable.

As advanced driver assistance systems (ADAS) and autonomous vehicles become more prevalent, understanding how well these systems can "read" our roads is crucial. ViaSight's Lane Analytics provides infrastructure owners and operators (IOOs) with powerful insights into how their roads perform with modern vehicle systems, helping ensure safety and readability for both human drivers and autonomous systems.
Lifelong founder and entrepreneur of tech from mobile to autonomous.  Decades of applied research and testing for ADAS and automated driving systems.  Trained in Functional Safety (ISO 26262). Graduate from the University of Minnesota (1982).  Active member Minnesota’s Council for Connected and Automated Vehicles (CAV-X).   Served on the Governor's council since 2019.

Before founding VSI and ViaSight, Phil was co-founder and president of Telematics Research Group (2001), which was acquired by iSuppli in 2008.  Phil led the Automotive Business Unit at iSuppli until it was acquired by IHS in 2011 where he was Senior Director for Automotive Technology.  In his tenure, Phil acquired a deep understanding of automotive electronics systems including active safety, connected vehicle, and automated driving technologies. Phil is the author of numerous reports and papers for industry trade publications as well as a frequent speaker at global automotive conferences and symposiums.

Pioneering a New Era of Innovation, Hope, and Life-Changing Solutions in Hearing Care
Empowering Lives Through Advanced Hearing Therapies
Hearing loss doesn’t have to be the end of the story. At Rescue Hearing, we’re developing breakthrough gene therapies that could actually restore hearing—not just manage symptoms. We combine cutting-edge science with a genuine passion to create change, working toward a future where hearing loss may no longer be permanent. This is more than research; it’s a mission to transform how we think about hearing care forever. Come join us on this journey.

Our Story
A Mission to Restore Hearing

Rescue Hearing is we are committed to revolutionizing the treatment of hearing loss through cutting-edge gene therapies. With over 466 million people worldwide affected by hearing impairment, and no approved therapies as of 2023, our mission is clear: to develop life-changing solutions that restore hearing function and improve quality of life. RHI was born from a personal journey—CEO Jim Ayala’s drive to find a cure for his son Xavier’s genetic hearing loss.

With a team of leading physicians, researchers, and genetic specialists, we are at the forefront of innovation, leveraging gene therapy, small molecule imaging, and advanced diagnostics to combat hearing loss caused by genetic mutations. With over 100 known genetic causes of hearing loss, every breakthrough we achieve brings us closer to a world where hearing impairment is no longer a lifelong challenge. Through strategic investments and innovative research, RHI is paving the way for transformative hearing restoration therapies.

The RHI Vision
Our vision is to eliminate hearing loss as a chronic condition through groundbreaking advancements in gene therapy and medical innovation. We are committed to a future where genetic hearing loss is no longer a lifelong challenge but a treatable condition, giving millions the chance to experience the world of sound.

The RHI Mission
We are developing a portfolio of precision-engineered gene therapy products including DFNB8 (licensed to Myrtelle) and USH1B/DFNB16, leveraging advanced biotechnology and molecular diagnostics to create targeted hearing restoration treatments. Through innovation and expertise, we strive to create targeted treatments that restore hearing and transform lives worldwide.

A Mission Rooted in Personal Experience
Image 82 - Rescue Hearing
Jim Ayala

Founder & CEO

As a father of a son born with genetic hearing loss, Jim Ayala founded Rescue Hearing Inc. with a mission to revolutionize hearing care. Driven by personal experience and a deep commitment to making a difference, he set out to bridge the gap between medical research and real-world solutions.

A Father’s Journey: From Diagnosis to Innovation
Image 758 - Rescue Hearing
Image 379 - Rescue Hearing
Early Life & Discovery
In 2005, Xavier was born. As with most newborns, he received a newborn hearing screening, which he did not pass. Xavy’s parents, as would most, were confused and unsure of where to go from there. Over the next four years, Xavier experienced chronic ear infections, received multiple medications, and made many trips to hearing specialists. None of the clinicians were able to explain the chronic ear infections or the cause of Xavier’s hearing loss. Despite his congenital hearing loss, Xavy was exceptionally bright, insatiably curious, and did not experience any speech or language delays.

2010 (age 5) Xavy was diagnosed with Bilateral Sensorineural Nonsyndromic Hearing Loss (SNHI). He was fitted for hearing aids that would be required for the rest of his life.

2015 (age 10) Through the persistence and determination of his parents to find the cause (and potential cure) for Xavy’s hearing loss, genetic testing was discovered. Genetic testing is usually available to children born completely deaf but not to those children who are born with residual hearing (like Xavy). Xavy’s parents pushed and paid out of pocket for a blood sample to be sent to a lab that specialized in genetic testing for hearing loss. His parents did not know that between 30% to 50% of all hearing loss is caused by genetic defects and that there are over 100 genetic mutations known to cause hearing loss. The results of Xavy’s genetic test revealed a defect in the STRC gene. The STRC gene is responsible for building functional inner ear hair cells. Mutations in the STRC gene can cause misformed hair cells that can result in congenital hearing loss or deafness.

Xavier’s Journey: Defying Limits
Rescuehearing Images
Growing Up & Overcoming Challenges
Despite his hearing loss, Xavy never let it define him. With the support of his four siblings and his community, he actively participated in sports, theater, robotics, and academic pursuits.

🏅 2016-2020 (Middle School): Little league baseball, lacrosse, football, theater, summer camps, and robotics.
🎭 2021-2023 (High School): Drama, golf, competitive dance, and excelling in engineering, physics, and math.

Xavy’s insatiable curiosity and passion for innovation have led him to explore automotive engineering and racing, fields where precision and cutting-edge technology merge.

Race to Hear: Merging Passion with Purpose
In 2022, Xavier founded Race to Hear (RTH), a non-profit organization dedicated to using automotive engineering demonstrations to educate children about how cars work.

What RTH Does:
Live go-kart racing demonstrations at summer camps, community events, and libraries.

Sponsorships from local businesses, charities, and donors to support the cause.

Funds raised directly contribute to providing hearing aids for underprivileged individuals.

Im2age 86 - Rescue Hearing
Looking Ahead: A Bright Future
The breakthrough journey!

In 2023, Xavier will graduate at the top of his class and embark on his academic journey at Harvard University (Class of 2028). His story is a testament to the fact that hearing loss should never limit potential.

With determination, innovation, and breakthrough science, the future is limitless. At Rescue Hearing Inc., we are committed to eliminating hearing loss as a chronic condition—because everyone deserves the power of sound.

We are committed to shaping a brighter future for  administrators, teachers, and students - helping to shape the new standard for education.


Our story
Our co-founders struggled tremendously in education growing up. 



Kevin Celisca, a first-generation Haitian American, found himself on the brink of failing fourth grade, confronted by the barriers of limited access to resources and support. It was the dedication of a teacher who saw his potential, tailored lessons to cater to his unique needs, and provided free tutoring every day after school that turned his life around. This experience wasn't just transformative; it was a revelation.



Similarly, Maxwell Witt faced his own battles with a learning disability that threatened to derail his educational journey. The intervention of a dedicated tutor was pivotal, offering him the strategies and support he needed to succeed.



We know that not every child is so lucky, which is why we founded our company with a singular, unwavering mission: to democratize the kind of personalized educational support that Kevin and Maxwell were so fortunate to receive. We believe every student deserves a champion - a teacher or tutor who can tailor education to their individual needs, recognize their potential, and guide them towards success, regardless of their background or challenges.

The Integrate School Difference
We know that too many personal hours are spent creating lesson plans, with too little data and minimal feedback for quality assurance. We built Integrate school so that teachers can have high-quality lesson plans in just a few clicks of a button, so they can spend more time on really matters, teaching.



We work on this mission daily because we want to make a high standard of education accessible to everyone. Reach-out Message: Hi Andrea, Building Willowfinch around location intelligence and personal finance reflects thoughtful use of data for real-life decision making. What helped you translate complex housing, tax, and insurance data into a practical user experience? Looking forward to connecting. Parag Google | Founder SoftwareBrio.com
Review Text: Andrea Brice, Founder of Willowfinch, shared a highly detailed and technically thoughtful review of Lunarbyte.io’s work on the frontend development and architecture of her data intelligence platform. Willowfinch was built to help people make more informed decisions about where and when they should retire by analyzing cost-of-living factors such as housing, taxes, health insurance, real estate data, and personal financial details. Andrea had strong expertise in data science and backend logic, but needed a capable frontend and architecture partner to build the user-facing side of the product while supporting security, privacy, authentication, and fintech-related requirements. Lunarbyte.io was brought in to serve not only as a development partner but also as a functional CTO, helping guide frontend architecture and product decisions for the MVP. Their work included creating the frontend architecture, integrating authentication, building a development environment, and preparing the system to pass data between the frontend and backend. The product flow involved users browsing real estate websites, saving properties through a bookmarklet, triggering API requests for geocoded data, and returning details such as property taxes, assessed values, bedrooms, bathrooms, and location-specific affordability information. That data would then connect with Andrea’s backend models to calculate state and local income taxes, health insurance estimates, and cost differences over a 10-year planning window. Lunarbyte.io also helped shift the architecture away from a Chrome widget toward a bookmarklet, showing strong technical judgment. Andrea emphasized that Lunarbyte.io got the interface functional and helped establish a privacy-first architecture where sensitive information and identifiable data were separated across databases. She praised their project management, consistency, communication, and ability to set realistic expectations. Her advisor became comfortable stepping away from managing the team because Lunarbyte.io handled tasks, tracking, and communication so effectively. Andrea highlighted Francisco’s leadership and James’s technical partnership, noting that James listened carefully, challenged assumptions when needed, explained tradeoffs clearly, and helped ideate practical compromises. She was especially impressed by Lunarbyte.io’s security awareness, technical depth, and ability to handle complex frontend requirements tied to sensitive financial and personal data. Overall, she described the collaboration as productive, educational, and enjoyable, with confidence that Lunarbyte.io would deliver a superior-quality product and help guide the company toward success.

About
Willowfinch was inspired by Andrea’s own experience navigating the decision of where to retire.

Review Text: Peter Brach, Founder of Propel Philanthropy, shared a positive and quality-focused experience working with Sunlight Media LLC on a website redesign project for his nonprofit organization. Propel Philanthropy focuses on strengthening social impact infrastructure by supporting organizations that drive large-scale change across philanthropy, nonprofit ecosystems, and civil society. The goal of the engagement was to create a high-end, professional website design that would resonate with upper-middle-class, wealthy, and ultra-wealthy audiences, aligning with the organization’s positioning and mission. Sunlight Media LLC was selected because of their ability to deliver the level of professional design quality the client was seeking. The project involved a structured and collaborative design process, beginning with the creation of approximately eight logo options. After selecting a preferred direction, Peter requested revisions, which the team handled efficiently. The team then presented three homepage design options, and once one was chosen, they worked closely with the client through multiple iterations to refine the design until it fully met expectations. Following this, Sunlight Media designed four additional pages, again incorporating feedback and revisions to ensure consistency and quality across the site. The final product was reviewed on their WordPress server before being migrated to the client’s server, along with an instruction manual to support ongoing management. The engagement also included additional post-launch support, with the team responding to further requests without additional charges. Peter highlighted that the project was executed smoothly from start to finish, with clear communication and effective project management. He specifically emphasized the high quality of the design work and described the team as high-end professionals, noting that their level of design expertise stood out compared to many other providers. While he mentioned that clients should consider the trade-offs between template-based and custom design approaches, he did not identify any major shortcomings in the engagement. Overall, the project successfully delivered a polished, professional website aligned with the organization’s brand and audience expectations, reinforcing Sunlight Media LLC’s reputation for high-quality design and client-focused execution.

Propel Philanthropy is a network of 50-plus organizations that provide infrastructure within philanthropy and other sectors. We convene, inform, and inspire funders to focus on highly effective and efficient mechanisms to hasten positive social change. We make known that a small increase in infrastructure funding can significantly fast-track social impact. Investments that unlock charitable capital, strengthen philanthropic support organizations and build the infrastructure that fosters collaboration driving exceptional social impact returns. These disproportionate returns can be realized at scale by investing in Social Impact Infrastructure Organizations (SIIOs).

Propel Philanthropy has coined the term SIIO to refer to organizations providing social impact-related infrastructure within “for good” sectors. Examples within our network or otherwise include GivingTuesday, VolunteerMatch, GoFundMe, Council of Nonprofits, National Council on Foundations, TechSoup, and Alliance Magazine.

About Us
Propel Philanthropy is grounded in the belief that some of the most transformative philanthropic impact comes from funding that accelerates the social sector itself. We provide free on-site curated listings that not only strengthen the sector but also help funders achieve highly leveraged, far-reaching results. We pay particular attention to promising social impact infrastructure organizations, recognizing that with the right support, some can deliver exceptional, sector-wide impact.

Propel Philanthropy’s work is guided by five convictions:

We must invest in social impact-related infrastructure. We cannot begin to estimate how much good it could do for the social sector. Conversely, we cannot calculate the opportunity loss we will incur if we don’t.
Failing to build robust infrastructure affects all of us. Had we made sufficient investments in infrastructure ten years ago, we’d now have far greater charitable and human capital, usable data, and other sector-wide resources. If we fail to invest moving forward, all of our efforts will be compromised.
The ROI funders can achieve by investing in infrastructure is extensive—and, in some cases, exponential. There are many examples where infrastructure funding has unlocked remarkable results.
Small grants sometimes drive big results. Some assume that it takes large grants to invest in infrastructure. But that is not always the case. As shown on our Stories of Impact page, small, strategic grants can transform organizations.
Investing in broad, far-reaching strategies can positively impact many lives, communities, causes, and environments. In theory—and often in practice—the wider the reach of the organization you support, the greater your impact. A capacity grant to a small nonprofit can often do more than improve a single program—it can strengthen the organization. Support for a coalition or network can amplify results for many members. Support for a social impact infrastructure organization can go even further: It can accelerate entire fields, sectors, and regions.
We Advocate for Increasing Social Sector Funding
Most notably, Propel Philanthropy partnered with Alliance Magazine and the Worldwide Initiative for Grantmaker Support (WINGS) on two critical campaigns. One drew considerable attention to the roles, aspirations, and challenges nine leading social impact infrastructure organization leaders faced. The second took a deep dive into why five funders supported these organizations. Together, these campaigns drew new awareness to the value of this subsector of organizations to tens of thousands of readers globally.

Learn More
Convening Funders
Propel Philanthropy convenes funder-to-funder meetings to accelerate the social sector. The more charitable capital we unlock, the bigger, more inclusive, and equitable pie we can create. The same principle applies broadly. We are delighted to share that we have brought together funders from major foundations to small private donors. We warmly invite all with their own endowments to join us. Meetings are held quarterly on Zoom and last an hour.

Join Us on Our Learning Journey

Explore More on Our Website

Directory of SIIOs
Browse our list of SIIOs that collectively strengthen the social sector across many cause areas and geographies.
Stories of Impact
See real examples where small, strategic infrastructure grants led to impressive results.
Listings for Funders
We provide funders with listings that we believe have the potential to bring about exceptional results by positively impacting many people, organizations, communities, or environments.
Get in Touch
With nearly six years of experience making successful, far-reaching infrastructure grants, Peter Brach is available to help funders identify highly leveraged, far-reaching opportunities. We invite you to explore how he can support you.

Review Text: Chris DeLuca, Owner of All Pro Window Tinting in Atlanta, shared a highly positive and long-term experience working with SolutionBuilt for SEO, SEM, and digital marketing services. The primary goal of the engagement was to increase the company’s visibility in a competitive local market and attract more customers through online channels. SolutionBuilt provided a comprehensive digital marketing solution, including search engine optimization, Google AdWords campaign management, and social media marketing across platforms such as Facebook, Instagram, and Twitter. Their work involved researching competitors, identifying high-impact keywords, and continuously refining strategies to ensure strong search performance. Over the course of the engagement, which began in 2006 and remains ongoing, SolutionBuilt played a critical role in helping All Pro Window Tinting achieve and maintain top rankings in search results. Chris highlighted that his company consistently ranks as the number one window tinting provider in Atlanta for key organic search terms and paid campaigns, which directly contributed to increased call volume and business growth. The team demonstrated strong project management by delivering work on time, providing monthly campaign analytics, and proactively adjusting strategies when performance metrics indicated the need for changes. Communication was frequent, with regular check-ins ranging from daily or weekly conversations to in-person meetings every six months. Review Text: Bomani Mintz-Johnson, former Founder and CEO of Crave It, shared a detailed and experience-driven review of Fullsend Solutions Inc. for their contribution to building a mobile application for a food lovers social networking platform. Crave It aimed to create a global community-driven app centered around food discovery, social interaction, and user-generated content. To bring this vision to life, Fullsend Solutions Inc. was engaged primarily for their frontend development expertise, while the internal team managed backend development, quality assurance, marketing, and product strategy. Carbon Ridge (“CR”) is a United States based developer of carbon capture & storage solutions for the Maritime industry. CR's Onboard Carbon Capture & Storage (“OCCS”) solution provides a low-cost and near-term solution to significantly reduce the intensity of carbon dioxide (CO2) and other greenhouse gases (GHGs) onboard large maritime vessels.

Carbon Ridge logo

Light blue shipping container with Carbon Ridge logo with white text on the side
Highly-Modular,

Capture Technology

Our OCCS module is mounted adjacent to the vessels exhaust funnel. Exhaust gas is routed into the OCCS module where the CO2, NOx & SOx are separated from the flue gas. Clean exhaust then flows back to the funnel and CO2-rich solvent is separated

Compression & Storage

Separated CO2 is sent to a second container where it is compressed, liquified and transferred to storage tank(s) for the remainder of the voyage.

We provide end-to-end decarbonization for shipping.
Transportation: CO2 is offloaded at port or on offshore terminals and transported to be permanently sequestered or utilized in value-added products.

Linecook is the app for passionate home cooks who want to celebrate every meal they create with their own two hands.

We’re building an AI-powered social app that helps you store kitchen memories and milestones, track your growth and skills in the kitchen, discover fresh ideas of what to cook, and strengthen the bonds that form over food, even when you can’t be at the same table.

Founded by Nick Kafker and Nick Stewart, who previously built Recover Athletics and sold it to Strava in 2022.

Our vision: to become the record of the world’s home cooked meals and the place where passionate home cooks come together to celebrate every meal they create.

About — Linecook
Linecook
About
Join the Beta
About Linecook
Nick Stewart and Nick Kafker, Linecook co-founders
Hi, I'm Nick. And I'm also Nick.

We've been doing this schtick since 2009, and we still get a kick out of it. Nick and I (see, you still don't know who's speaking) have been best friends since freshman year of high school and are excited to tell you about our second startup, Linecook.

Our first company, Recover Athletics, a physical therapy app for runners, sold to Strava in 2022. We love endurance sports, and we also love to cook. Raised by incredible home cooks (thanks, Moms!), we spent countless nights in our dingy Boston apartment making meals together while building Recover. Nothing fancy, lots of hot sauce, but those kitchen sessions were always the highlight of our days.

Even after we moved to SF, the habit stuck. We'd text photos of dishes we made, swap recipes, and fill our group chat with cooking banter. Eventually, we realized we were missing something: a dedicated place to save those cooking memories and milestones. We had Strava for our runs; we needed a Strava for our cooking: a spot to share, track progress, and celebrate the meals made with our own two hands.

In 2025, we left Strava to build exactly that. Linecook will be your favorite social app: a place to post your creations, track your growth in the kitchen, discover new ideas, and strengthen the bonds that form over food, even when you can't be around the same table.

Home cooks are our people. We can't wait to see what you're making.

Review Text: Jake Koppinger, CEO and Founder of FreightRoll, shared a highly positive and detailed review of Shrine Development, emphasizing their professionalism, efficiency, and ability to deliver a complex mobile application within a tight timeframe. He explained that FreightRoll operates as a digital freight marketplace, and the company required a robust iOS application to support its operations and enhance usability for truck drivers and logistics workflows. Jake highlighted that the application was not a simple build, but rather a reasonably complex system incorporating location-based services and operational features critical to the platform’s success. One of the most notable outcomes of the engagement was the speed of execution, with the majority of development completed in approximately one month while still maintaining high quality. The resulting application became a valuable operational asset, enabling easier product updates and improving overall workflow efficiency for users. From a project management standpoint, Jake emphasized that Shrine Development required minimal oversight, demonstrating strong autonomy and professionalism throughout the engagement. The team provided daily updates outlining completed work, outstanding tasks, and time spent, ensuring full transparency and alignment. Communication was seamless across email and Slack, allowing real-time collaboration when needed. He also appreciated their proactive approach, noting that if progress stalled in one area due to external dependencies, the team would immediately shift focus to other parts of the project to maintain momentum. Additionally, their ability to re-engage quickly after initial development and support final launch efforts further demonstrated their reliability. Jake also highlighted the quality of the codebase, mentioning that his CTO was particularly impressed with its cleanliness and maintainability, which would support future enhancements and audits. He did not identify any areas for improvement, indicating complete satisfaction with both the process and the outcome. Overall, his review reflects a successful collaboration where Shrine Development delivered a high-quality, scalable iOS application with speed, strong communication, and exceptional project management.

From a project management perspective, Clarion Technologies performed strongly, staying within budget and meeting deadlines in most cases, while maintaining clear communication through daily scrum meetings and collaboration tools like Microsoft Teams. What stood out most to Warren was their ability to deeply understand client requirements and ensure the right mix of resources was allocated to meet project needs effectively. Review Text: Alan Narz, President of Instant Evaluate, shared a highly detailed and long-term perspective on his collaboration with Clarion Technologies, highlighting their role as a core development partner for over a decade. He explained that Instant Evaluate focuses on process automation, offering solutions in automated customer service management and recruiting, including an advanced auto-scheduler system for interviews. The company initially struggled with scaling its development efforts due to inconsistent workloads and unreliable local resources. Clarion Technologies addressed this challenge by providing a flexible, scalable development model that allowed the team to expand or contract resources based on demand. One of their earliest and most impactful contributions was standardizing the platform architecture. Previously, Instant Evaluate built custom solutions for each client, which limited scalability. Clarion redesigned the system into a unified platform, making it significantly easier to onboard new clients and implement changes efficiently. The platform includes features such as IVR-based and online survey collection, real-time reporting, automated alerts for negative feedback, and social sharing prompts for positive experiences. On the recruitment side, the system enables candidates to apply through job links, answer qualification questions, and self-schedule interviews based on recruiter availability, with automated confirmations and reminders. Alan emphasized that Clarion handles all technical aspects, including PHP development, database management, and mobile-related components, allowing him to focus on business strategy despite not having a technical background. The partnership involves 3–4 full-time developers from Clarion, effectively functioning as the company’s entire development team. From a project management perspective, Clarion provides daily summaries of completed work, maintains consistent communication through email and project management tools, and ensures transparency in progress tracking. Alan noted that their estimates are generally accurate, although scope changes—common in custom software projects—can extend timelines. What stood out most to him was Clarion’s partner-oriented mindset, as they take genuine ownership of the product and share both the successes and challenges of the business. He expressed complete trust in their capabilities, stating that when Clarion confirms something works, it typically does. While minor challenges such as time zone differences and evolving requirements exist, they have not impacted the overall success of the engagement. Overall, Alan’s review reflects a deeply integrated and long-standing partnership where Clarion Technologies has played a critical role in building scalable, flexible, and high-performing automation platforms that continue to drive business growth.

Review Text: Stephanie Lauridsen Smith, Communications Manager at Wingate's Quality Watches, shared a highly detailed and experience-driven review of Hashrocket, highlighting their efficiency, responsiveness, and ability to resolve complex technical issues quickly. She explained that Wingate’s Quality Watches is a small retail company specializing in high-end watches, where she manages communications, design, and IT responsibilities. Prior to working with Hashrocket, the company struggled with unreliable freelance developers who lacked consistency and responsiveness, leading to delays in resolving critical website issues. The main challenge involved fixing a dual-system architecture where the frontend was built using Ruby on Rails and the backend was developed in PHP, which created synchronization and ordering issues. Hashrocket stepped in and quickly analyzed both systems, successfully enabling seamless communication between them and resolving core ordering problems within a short timeframe. One of the most significant outcomes of the engagement was the rapid pace of execution, with Stephanie noting that Hashrocket accomplished more in just three weeks than previous developers had achieved in years. The team also improved the user experience by enabling multiple image uploads for products, which allowed customers to better evaluate items and reduced the number of inquiries related to product clarity. From a project management standpoint, she emphasized the team’s exceptional organization and communication. They maintained daily calls, documented discussions thoroughly, and used tools like Basecamp and Pivotal Tracker to manage tasks and ensure transparency. This structured approach allowed Stephanie to track progress, revisit conversations, and stay aligned with development priorities. She particularly appreciated that nothing fell through the cracks, as the team consistently followed up on every detail discussed. While she mentioned a minor concern regarding invoice transparency, where more detailed breakdowns of billed hours would have been helpful, her overall feedback remained overwhelmingly positive. She strongly endorsed Hashrocket for their professionalism, technical capability, and ability to deliver high-quality solutions quickly. Overall, her review reflects a highly successful engagement where Hashrocket transformed a struggling e-commerce system into a more efficient, user-friendly platform through strong technical expertise, clear communication, and disciplined execution. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}

Review Text: Adam Justice, Vice President at Grid Connect, shared a detailed and thoughtful review of Hashrocket, highlighting their role in developing a foundational web-based platform that enabled communication and interaction with connected hardware devices. He explained that Grid Connect, traditionally a hardware-focused company, was entering the Internet of Things (IoT) space and needed a robust software layer to complement its Wi-Fi-enabled products. Since the organization lacked in-house expertise in web-based software development, they partnered with Hashrocket to design and build a platform using Ruby on Rails that could monitor and manage hardware systems effectively. One of the key aspects of the engagement was the collaborative discovery process, including a structured “story mapping” phase that ensured both teams were aligned on product vision and execution strategy. Adam emphasized that Hashrocket’s expertise, structured processes, and clear communication style were critical factors in establishing a strong working relationship. The team maintained daily video standups, which created a tight feedback loop and fostered a sense of unity despite geographic distance. This approach led to a highly collaborative environment where Hashrocket felt like an extension of the internal team rather than an external vendor. The platform was successfully launched and became a key component of the company’s IoT product offering, even being made available through major channels such as Amazon’s home automation marketplace. Customer feedback on the product has been very positive, validating both the functionality and usability of the system. Adam also noted that Grid Connect adopted some of Hashrocket’s development practices internally, demonstrating the lasting impact of their process and methodology. While he mentioned that clearer cost and timeline estimates for new feature additions would have been beneficial during evolving project scopes, this did not significantly impact overall satisfaction. Overall, his review reflects a successful partnership where Hashrocket delivered a technically strong and scalable platform while fostering close collaboration, strong communication, and process improvements that extended beyond the initial engagement. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}

Reach-out Message: Hi Martin, Building Spectra Technology’s safety and tracking systems for rally and circuit racing reflects strong execution in a highly specialized industry. What helped you translate complex race-control needs into user-friendly mobile tools? Looking forward to connecting. Parag Google | Founder SoftwareBrio.com
Service Outsourced: Custom Software Development & Mobile App Development
Review Text: Martin Huffmann, Partner and Co-CEO of Spectra Technology, shared a highly positive review of Robin, formerly Gonni Agency, emphasizing their ability to quickly understand a niche automotive racing safety industry and deliver technically strong mobile solutions. He explained that Spectra Technology develops safety devices, tracking systems, and communication tools for automotive racing, including rally and circuit competitions. The company engaged Robin to build two mobile applications compatible with both iOS and Android, along with web access for race-control staff. The first project focused on a rally racing application sponsored by FIA, requiring strict specifications, precise geolocation, communication features, and usability for multiple end-user groups including competitors and race-control teams. Robin interpreted the FIA requirements, adapted them to Spectra’s devices, and handled development, prototyping, testing, UI creation, database setup, and geolocation implementation. Martin highlighted that the final rally app was user-friendly, fluid, and reliable, with end users able to operate it with minimal training. A major achievement was FIA approval, which led to Spectra being listed as one of only three approved providers on the FIA website, while several rally institutions began using the app. He especially appreciated the quality of the geolocation and communication tools, noting that the app supported safety and coordination during live race conditions. From a project management standpoint, Robin met the strict deadlines for the first project and maintained a smooth workflow through weekly and later biweekly meetings. Their CTO remained closely involved, helping ensure technical alignment and consistent execution. Although the second project introduced more complexity and some timeline delays, Martin considered those delays understandable and remained satisfied with the collaboration. What impressed him most was Robin’s technical capability and their ability to grasp a highly specific industry quickly despite limited prior knowledge of auto racing. Overall, his review reflects a successful ongoing partnership where Robin delivered specialized, reliable, and user-friendly racing safety applications that strengthened Spectra Technology’s credibility and operational reach.

We developed the Square 1 Credit Suite® because as veterans of the commercial lending industry, we have been in your shoes.
 
Suntell was founded in 1996 by bankers, who were tired of the labor intensiveness of every single step of the collection and analyzing of financial data, of servicing a loan portfolio, and managing the collections of problem loans.  There were checklists for everything and duplicate data entry across multiple disparate systems.  

Our goal is to help community lending institutions of all sizes build a sound lending program through an integrated piece of software.  A single solution, for all lending functions.

As financial industry professionals, we have worked as lenders, as operations managers, as credit analysts, and as loan support staff.  We have made a commitment to delivering a superior commercial and agricultural loan risk management software solution.  Our success is in knowing that community banks and credit unions across the United States are benefiting from our products through better asset quality, strong safety and soundness regulatory examinations, and better workflow efficiency. 

We knew there had to be a better way.

That’s why we pioneered the Loan Management System (LMS) for maximum productivity.  From there, we added Suntell Document Imaging (SDI) for borrower, loan, and deposit documents; AgExam for agricultural analysis; Stress Tests to better assess your risk; and Loan Queue and Dashboards for workflow management.  Together, these components resulted in the Square 1 Credit Suite ® . We use our skills and subject matter expertise to continue to develop and enhance our products to be responsive to the industry and feedback from our valued customers.

Who is Suntell?
Our Story
Innovation Born from Experience. Driven by What’s Next.
Suntell was founded in 1996 by commercial lenders who saw firsthand how outdated, manual processes were slowing down lending teams. From duplicative data entry to disconnected systems and endless checklists, managing a loan portfolio was far too complex—and far from strategic.

They knew there had to be a smarter way.

So they built it.

Suntell pioneered the first Loan Management System (LMS) designed to streamline commercial and agricultural lending from start to finish. But we didn’t stop there. We listened to our customers, anticipated the industry’s evolution, and continued to build.

We added tools like:

Suntell Document Imaging (SDI) to centralize borrower, loan, and deposit documentation,
AgExam for in-depth agricultural analysis,
Stress Testing to proactively assess risk, and
Loan Queue & Dashboards to streamline workflows and boost team performance.
And we didn’t stop there.

These additions—and many more—have evolved into the Square 1 Credit Suite®, an innovative, ever-advancing platform that continues to grow with the needs of our customers and the changing demands of the industry.


Our Mission
Our mission is to empower community lending institutions to build strong, scalable, and compliant commercial and agricultural lending programs—through one powerful, integrated solution.

linkedin company about:
A multimedia division of the Annenberg Foundation, explore™ is a multimedia organization that champions the selfless acts of others, documenting leaders around the world who have devoted their lives to extraordinary causes. Both educational and inspirational, explore films cover topics from animal rights, health and human services, and poverty to the environment, education, and spirituality. explore.org also hosts one of the largest online networks of live HD nature and animal cameras.

Specialties
Documentary films, photography, and inspiration and knowledgeAbout Us
 


explore is a multimedia organization that documents leaders around the world who have devoted their lives to extraordinary causes. Both educational and inspirational, explore creates a portal into the soul of humanity by championing the selfless acts of others.

explore’s growing library consists of more than 250 original films and 30,000 photographs from around the world. We showcase our work at film festivals, on over 100 public broadcast and cable channels, and on numerous online destinations including explore.org, Snag Films, Hulu and TakePart.

“At explore we are archivists,” explains founder Charles Annenberg Weingarten. “We strive to create films that allow the viewer to join us on our journey as we go on location and experience what unfolds. It’s like the viewers are traveling with the team.”

explore features a wide range of topics—from animal rights, health and human services, and poverty to the environment, education, and spirituality. Delivered in short, digestible bites, explore films appeal to viewers of all ages, from children learning about other cultures for the first time to adults looking for a fresh perspective on the world around them.

explore films include: a woman in Mumbai who has dedicated her life to rescuing young women from prostitution, a priest in Los Angeles who helps gang members reintegrate into society, a project to protect wild mountain gorillas in Rwanda, and an environmental university in Costa Rica that focuses on sustainable development.

Individuals and organizations featured in many explore films also receive explore funding in the form of Annenberg Foundation grants. To date, over $69 million has been awarded to over 300 non-profit organizations worldwide.

explore.org, the online portal for explore, is a community destination where people share thoughts, engage in dialogue, view and email films and photographs, and embed their favorites on blogs and social networking sites.

Never stop learning™. Come explore.

Our Mission
To champion the selfless acts of others, create a portal into the soul of humanity and inspire lifelong learning.

Explore.org is the world's leading philanthropic live nature cam network and documentary film channel.

What is SHWASH?
SHWASH is a mobile on-demand car detailing service. We strive to provide the most convenient, reliable and transparent service to allow you to never have to worry about how you’re going to keep your car clean again. We bring the detail right to your doorstep, at the click of a button.
How do I book an appointment?
The process is simple. It starts with entering your address so we know where we will be detailing the vehicle, selecting the detailing package that best suits your needs,  followed by choosing a convenient day/time for you based on our availability for your area. Once the appointment is booked and confirmed, Your Detailer will come to your preferred location to provide the service on the day/time you requested. Rest assured, SHWASH securely handles your payment and chargers your card only after the service is completed, ensuring a secure transaction.
Do I need to call to get a quote first?
No you don’t! At SHWASH, we offer up-front and cost-effective pricing for our car detailing services. We have preset - fully transparent pricing via our select detail packages we offer. No matter the condition of your vehicle, there is a package that will fit your needs. If you are unsure which package to select, you can always contact us and we can recommend one for you based on your vehicle condition and what you want done :)
Do I need to book online?
All bookings are made online at https://book.shwash.co/. If you have a unique circumstance or would like someone on our team to book your appointment for you, please give us a call and we can assist you.
How do I pay?
Payment is done on the platform after entering your address, selecting your package, choosing the day/time of the appointment and providing your details. We accept both debit and credit cards for secure payment, ensuring utmost safety and security. Rest assured, SHWASH securely handles your payment and charges your card only after the service is completed, ensuring a secure transaction.

Review Text: Of A Kind, a fashion and home e-commerce company known for blending storytelling with commerce, partnered with Big Human to redevelop and scale its Shopify-based platform over a multi-year engagement. The project began with an existing platform that required significant enhancement to support business growth, improved user experience, and operational efficiency. Big Human took a comprehensive approach, rebuilding and optimizing the e-commerce infrastructure while aligning it closely with the company’s unique content-driven retail model. Their team, consisting of a project manager, designers, and developers, worked collaboratively to ensure the platform could support both transactional functionality and rich storytelling elements that defined the brand. Throughout the four-year partnership, Big Human demonstrated exceptional project management capabilities, utilizing structured communication channels such as Jira, Slack, email, phone calls, and regular meetings to maintain transparency and alignment. Their proactive and thoughtful approach stood out, as they invested time in deeply understanding the intricacies of Of A Kind’s business model, enabling them to deliver solutions that addressed both technical and strategic challenges holistically. This alignment contributed directly to measurable business growth during the engagement, with the platform evolving into a more robust, scalable, and user-friendly system. The collaboration was marked by consistent on-time delivery, high-quality execution, and a strong partnership dynamic, with Big Human acting not just as a vendor but as a strategic contributor to the company’s success. ```

Review Text: Unique Care Connect, a nonprofit organization dedicated to supporting families of children with special needs, partnered with KitelyTech to rapidly develop a professional, budget-friendly website that could establish credibility and enable fundraising efforts. Operating under tight financial constraints after allocating most of its initial funding toward mobile app development, the organization required a high-quality website delivered within an aggressive timeline. Through KitelyTech’s Startup Scholarship program, the client received expedited services at a significantly reduced cost, along with personalized support throughout the engagement. The project scope included designing and developing a fully functional website capable of presenting organizational content, capturing newsletter subscriptions, and facilitating online donations via PayPal. KitelyTech assembled a multidisciplinary team, including leadership oversight, project management, design, and backend engineering, to ensure seamless execution. The design phase was particularly impactful, as the team successfully translated the founder’s vision into a polished and professional interface on the first iteration. Development was completed in just five weeks—ahead of the six-week deadline—demonstrating strong project management and responsiveness, with consistent communication via Slack and email. The resulting website significantly enhanced the organization’s operational capabilities, enabling it to begin receiving private donations, grow its mailing list, and apply for both federal and private grants. This digital presence also improved the organization’s legitimacy when approaching larger partners and stakeholders. Overall, KitelyTech delivered a high-quality solution that not only met but exceeded expectations, playing a critical role in supporting the nonprofit’s mission and long-term sustainability.
linkedin company about:
Throughout out the majority of my life I have had the opportunity to observe all the nuances and challenges that come with raising a child with special needs. I have always felt like there could be more done for the children and their families. In the mass quarantines of 2020 while the world seemed to stand still, I decided it was time to start something new. That is when I had the idea for Unique Care Connect. Our mission at Unique Care Connect is to better the lives of children with special needs and their families, by providing customized resources in a neat organized way that families can access in a swift easy manner. As of June 2021 Unique Care Connect is a 501-C3 Nonprofit

Our current project that we are working on is the development of a mobile app whose primarily objective will be to help fix the respite care system. In addition, as a secondary objective UCC wants to build the first virtual platform for the special needs community that connects families to various resources that can assist families for what ever may arise.
